Titulo: Senior Analyst - BCG Vantage - Energy - Power & Gas Retail
Descripcion_Completa:
Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


What You'll Do

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Topic Activation path within BCG's cross topic Energy Practice Area you will work in a growing global team, providing industry expertise and insights whilst working together with case and proposal teams to provide knowledge assets, analysis and expert advisory. You’ll serve as a thought partner and content expert to case teams and topic leaders, helping structure and solve complex issues that will range significantly as per our client needs and latest developments and trends.

You will support commercialization efforts for the topic working, in conjunction with business leaders, through contributing analysis and insights to proposals, client workshops and marketing materials.


Additionally, as a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage, you will contribute to developing intellectual property & assets for the business and assist in onboarding and training junior colleagues based on your topic expertise.


Most of your time will be spent supporting case teams and developing Energy related content, working side by side with members of different backgrounds. You will dedicate time to further develop and deepen BCG’s expertise in these areas, and to support the Energy Practice, BCG Directors and Partners in business development initiatives.


BCG's Energy Practice Area help companies navigate an increasingly complex business climate.


In Energy we cover the Oil and Power & Gas sectors, including all "Green Energy" and Social Impact, Climate and Sustainability sectors. We work with the full range of players in the industry: integrated international oil companies (IOCs), national oil companies (NOCs), upstream players, oilfield service companies, refiners, petrochemical producers, and oil trading firms, integrated power and gas utilities, international power producers, renewable specialists, energy wholesalers and retailers, commodity traders, and infrastructure and industry service providers, water utilities and waste-management firms, governments, regulators, industry associations, NGOs, technology companies, OEMs and suppliers to the energy and environment industry, energy-intense heavy industry players.


YOU'RE GOOD AT

Solving client problems through formulating relevant research and/or analytical approaches in power and gas retail including and new products and services opportunity for utilities. Advanced excel modelling skills and high quantitative abilities are critical in this job position
Applying topic expertise when contributing to building and deploying a product/tool/data, taking different client contexts into account
Codifying knowledge and maintaining assets and tools
Working closely with topic leadership in defining business agenda
Being a self-starter, a proactive problem-solver who thrives in environments with minimal guidance and takes ownership of scoping and driving work forward. Intrapreneurial mindset highly valued
Communicating with case teams and stakeholders, in a credible and confident way
Working collaboratively and effectively in a group dynamically, often virtual, proficient in agile ways of working
Being flexible and bringing a curious and creative mindset, open to new things and able to propose innovative ideas
Navigating complexity and ambiguity
High critical thinking and being able to easily sense check with back of the envelope and quality checks analysis the quality of our messaging and outputs

RESPONSIBILITIES


Client Delivery

Leverages knowledge/experience to partner with consulting teams to identify and address critical knowledge needs e.g., what talent/skills are needed to drive the energy transition
Provides customized knowledge for client work, prepares accurate, well-developed analysis, in Office based formats
Delivers original analysis and insights to consulting team, provides thought partnership
Liaises with Research team to ensure that requests for expertise are addressed by Vantage and appropriate research is delivered

Topic and Practice Development

With consultants, collects, synthesizes, and leverages case team learning & inputs into new knowledge products (vignettes, experience)
Builds & updates new topic/sector-related customized knowledge products and tools
Controls Knowledge Management databases consistency
Reviews knowledge products & ensures they are current & relevant and updates when appropriate
Writes abstracts & indexes projects & PA documents
Collaborates on practice area intellectual capital development
Contributes to content development around topic/sector for PA meetings & trainings


What You'll Bring

2+ years of consulting in power and gas retail and/or power flexibility/demand response topics; candidates with consulting experience strongly preferred
In lieu of consulting experience, 3+ years minimum industry experience required; 4-6+ years of industry experience strongly preferred
Bachelor's Degree required (advanced degree preferred)
Fluency in English
Strong business acumen and problem-solving capabilities
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Outstanding interpersonal and communication skills to interact with internal and external stakeholder while working in a global collaborative team environment


Who You'll Work With

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Topic Activation path, you will collaborate with global topic leads and colleagues across BCG to bring expertise and capabilities to the service of our internal and external clients. Our team is made up of a diverse pool of BCG Vantage positions that allow us to pursue exciting, innovative BCG Vantage careers.


Additional info

BCG Vantage is a powerhouse of expertise, seamlessly embedded within Practice Areas and markets to drive extraordinary impact. It equips BCG case teams and clients with cutting-edge expertise, proprietary assets, actionable data, and transformative insights that empower our case teams and enable our clients to achieve their aspirations.


With global experts across 20 Practices, covering more than 100 topics, sectors and offers, plus four focused geographic markets, BCG Vantage delivers unparalleled depth and breadth of insight & expertise. BCG Vantage consists of three career paths, each playing a crucial role in delivering value to clients, case teams and practice teams.


Client Focus (Embedded Expertise)

Integrates into client-facing teams to deliver direct client impact through expertise, know-how, and proprietary tools and assets


Topic Activation (Sector Expertise with Proprietary Tool & Data Solutions)

Combines deep domain insights with our know-how and proprietary assets to address targeted challenges directly with clients and project teams; co-creates insights on industry trends and functional topics, proprietary data and tools, and teams to codify IP that all enables BCG to deliver excellent business value with its clients


Research (Practice Topic and Sector-Aligned Research and Insights)

Delivers actionable research and insights tailored to the client project’s needs; works with qualitative and quantitative data, conducts research and provides synthesized results from sources including commercial data, public and open source, company and industry research, macro-economic, commodities and trade data.

Titulo: Airbus UpNext Spain - E2E Bench Manager (m/f)
Descripcion_Completa:
Job Description:

At Airbus UpNext, we fly future technologies at speeds well beyond what seems feasible today. We're looking for passionate individuals to join a new demonstrator project that is shaping the future of sustainable aviation by advancing the aircraft electrification roadmap.

This project will focus on the challenges and opportunities of High Voltage Electrical Systems , which are essential for next-generation, low-emission aircraft part of Airbus ambition to electrify the skies.

As part of a fast-paced, agile team, you will help create a unique, state-of-the-art test facility from the ground up. Your mission will be to explore novel HV technologies at their limits and beyond, generating invaluable data and learnings needed to build the robust and reliable aircraft of tomorrow by safely testing the unsafe.

If you have a burning desire to make an impact and want to work in a startup-like environment backed by the strength of Airbus, this is your opportunity to help us decarbonize the aerospace industry .

This position is based in Getafe ( Spain), and it comes with a limited contract linked to the deliverable of the demonstrator (estimated date end of April 2028)

Mission:

As the E2E Bench Manager, reporting directly to the Technical Director, you own the Industrial Test Means pillar. You will act as a technical leader and people manager, guiding a dedicated team of engineers to deliver. You are responsible for the conception, design, manufacturing, installation and commissioning of the complex industrial electrical and mechanical equipment that constitutes the test facility in time and quality. You will ensure the facility can generate, distribute and dissipate HV power - up to 1500 VDC - while safely containing destructive failure events.

Your Main Responsibilities:

Team Leadership: Lead a high-performing team of 2-3 UpNext Test Means Engineers. You will define objectives, manage their workload and foster a “safety-first” culture within your squad.

Define the Test Bench Needs : Based on System Architectures provided by System Design Office and Test Intentions given by the E2E Test Manager, specify needed test bench requirements.

Test Means Development : Detailing the electrical and mechanical architecture and design of the bench. Lead the manufacturing and build of the facility core test capabilities including the electrical industrial equipment and the supporting systems linked to it and the test functions required, like industrial cooling, safe containers, environmental chambers, mechanical installation, UUT power wiring & signal cabling, Test Support System control / acquisition interconnection, etc.

Electrical Safety & Operations : Perform risk analysis and establish all required documentation for human safety related to your perimeter. Define the operational procedures for the test bench. Ensure compliance with electrical safety standards and industrial regulations. You will act as the primary authority for procedures and safety during test means commissioning. Oversee the physical integration of the Test Cells, ensuring the proper integration between the building infrastructure and the test equipment.

Supplier Identification & Steering : Study the market selecting potential subcontractors. Perform Make / Buy trade-offs. Support call for tenders and negotiate technical proposals. Manage technical performance of external and internal suppliers delivering heavy electrical equipment and custom test rigs, ensuring adherence to Time, Cost, Quality objectives up to on-site acceptance test and in-service support activities.

In-Service Continuous Operability : Ensure by continuous improvements a high test means operational reliability once the Entry Into Service milestone is achieved. Plan, define and perform required maintenance tasks and define obsolescence management.

Risks & Opportunities : Identify and analyze risks & opportunities affecting E2E bench performance and/or capacity, implementing corrective and preventive actions agreed within stop/go reviews.

Titulo: Senior Analyst - BCG Vantage, Airline Network Strategy & Planning
Descripcion_Completa:
Locations: London | Madrid | Paris | Frankfurt

Who We Are

Boston Consulting Group partners with leaders in business and society to tackle their most important challenges and capture their greatest opportunities. BCG was the pioneer in business strategy when it was founded in 1963. Today, we help clients with total transformation-inspiring complex change, enabling organizations to grow, building competitive advantage, and driving bottom-line impact.

To succeed, organizations must blend digital and human capabilities. Our diverse, global teams bring deep industry and functional expertise and a range of perspectives to spark change. BCG delivers solutions through leading-edge management consulting along with technology and design, corporate and digital ventures—and business purpose. We work in a uniquely collaborative model across the firm and throughout all levels of the client organization, generating results that allow our clients to thrive.


What You'll Do

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage focused on Airline Network Strategy & Planning, you will serve as a go-to subject matter expert on airline network design, partnering closely with BCG case teams and industry leaders to shape analytically rigorous, commercially grounded recommendations.


You will act as a thought partner to case teams and sector/topic leaders, supporting proposal efforts, client engagements, IP development, and tool design. In this role, you will:

Be BCG's internal go-to expert on airline network strategy, helping case teams understand how airline networks really work and how to make sound network decisions
Spot gaps in BCG’s airline network knowledge, tools, and materials, and take ownership of filling those gaps by creating new analyses, frameworks, tools, and slide decks
Design and evaluate airline network strategies, such as which routes to fly, where to grow or shrink capacity, how networks should evolve over time, and how airlines should respond to demand, cost, and competitive changes
Work with consulting teams to turn network mechanics into clear strategy, making sure recommendations are practical, economically sound, and grounded in real airline constraints
Build and improve reusable network planning tools, including models and GenAI-enabled solutions that case teams can apply across multiple client engagements
Create executive-ready materials (slides, benchmarks, playbooks) that explain network strategy clearly for senior airline and consulting leaders
Join airline client meetings, working sessions, and calls to provide network strategy expertise and support ongoing analyses


YOU'RE GOOD AT

Structuring and solving complex airline network planning problems, particularly those involving tradeoffs between connectivity, utilization, profitability, and operational constraints
Translating detailed schedule- and network-level analyses into clear strategic insights for senior stakeholders
Working hands-on with data, models, and planning tools rather than purely conceptual analyses
Applying strong commercial judgment to ambiguous planning decisions with real financial impact
Collaborating across global teams in fast-paced, high-expectation environments


What You'll Bring

3–6+ years of direct airline network planning experience, such as:
Network planning or network strategy roles at a commercial airline, with exposure to route portfolio design, capacity allocation, and network evolution decisions
OR top-tier consulting experience with substantial hands-on exposure to airline network planning topics
Demonstrated experience with:
O&D demand analysis and route-level profitability
Schedule design and seasonal planning cycles
Route and frequency planning
Fleet and aircraft assignment tradeoffs
Hands-on experience with airline planning tools such as NetLine/Plan, Sabre AirVision, Amadeus Sky Suite, or similar platforms (direct usage strongly preferred)
Strong understanding of airline economics, network effects, scheduling constraints, and capacity planning logic (Experience primarily focused on crew planning or rostering is less relevant for this role.)
Bachelor’s degree required; Master’s degree preferred (e.g., in Engineering, Operations Research, Data Science, or Transportation Economics)
Interest in or experience applying advanced analytics and GenAI to network optimization and decision support
Comfort operating in a consulting environment, including owning slide creation and executive messaging for senior stakeholders based on detailed analytical work
Strong business judgment, analytical rigor, and interpersonal skills
Fluency in English; additional language skills are a plus


Who You'll Work With

As a Senior Analyst – BCG Vantage on our Topic Activation path, you will collaborate with global topic leads and colleagues across BCG to bring expertise and capabilities to the service of our internal and external clients. Our team is made up of a diverse pool of BCG Vantage positions that allow us to pursue exciting, innovative BCG Vantage careers.


Additional info

BCG Vantage is a powerhouse of expertise, seamlessly embedded within Practice Areas and markets to drive extraordinary impact. It equips BCG case teams and clients with cutting-edge expertise, proprietary assets, actionable data, and transformative insights that empower our case teams and enable our clients to achieve their aspirations.

With global experts across 20 Practices, covering more than 100 topics, sectors and offers, plus four focused geographic markets, BCG Vantage delivers unparalleled depth and breadth of insight & expertise. BCG Vantage consists of three career paths, each playing a crucial role in delivering value to clients, case teams and practice teams.


Client Focus (Embedded Expertise)

Integrates into client-facing teams to deliver direct client impact through expertise, know-how, and proprietary tools and assets


Topic Activation (Sector Expertise with Proprietary Tool & Data Solutions)

Combines deep domain insights with our know-how and proprietary assets to address targeted challenges directly with clients and project teams; co-creates insights on industry trends and functional topics, proprietary data and tools, and teams to codify IP that all enables BCG to deliver excellent business value with its clients


Research (Practice Topic and Sector-Aligned Research and Insights)

Delivers actionable research and insights tailored to the client project’s needs; works with qualitative and quantitative data, conducts research and provides synthesized results from sources including commercial data, public and open source, company and industry research, macro-economic, commodities and trade data.

Monitor performance/note deficiencies of service contractors and recommend changes as necessary

Proven experience in the operation, installation, and maintenance of data center critical equipment or other critical facility environments.
Knowledge of the electrical and mechanical systems such as Generators, Switchgear, UPS systems, ATS/STS units, PDU/PMM units, Chillers, Air handling units, CRAC units and building management systems (BMS)
Good verbal and written communication English & Spanish
Electrical qualification
Ability to write and review operational documents such as SOPs, MOPs, EOPs

Job Description:

An opportunity for MRTT Industrial Quality Support has arisen within Airbus Operations in Getafe (Madrid).

You will ensure that the company’s operational processes (develop, fulfil, support and source) comply with the quality assurance requirements, standards and internal or third party certification/approvals as defined in the Quality Management System (QMS) and quality plan for hardware/software/services.

You will devise and validate the hardware/software/services' control and inspection plan and ensure its proper implementation (using traditional and new methods, e.g. virtual/augmented reality).

You will monitor the conformity & attestation status.

Where deviations occur, you will be involved in the handling of the non- conformance including analysis of the deviations, monitoring of the corrective and preventive actions, as well as contributing to the improvement of the quality standards.

As such, you will collect, analyze, visualize and interpret the relevant process and/or in service performance data using available means (manual and automatic) to identify early process variations and to take effective actions by means of root cause analysis to mitigate and resolve any quality issues by supporting a robust PPS method application.

You will provide the evidence of conformity to processes and requirements to Programme Quality Assurance to ensure the satisfaction of customer requirements.

In new product / process / service development you will ensure that operational processes' lessons learnt and risk analysis are done early, and that relevant preventive quality standards are used in order to mitigate the risks and that a rigorous Development Plan (multi- functional reviews, maturity gates, ...) is followed.

You will implement & operate Prevention Means (such as APQP, DFMEA) . In case of software development you will ensure software and data integrity, verify and validate/certify the software and data in compliance with the appropriate standard for its specific application, e.g. on-board software, ground test software for prototype, small series production, simulation models, decision making support systems.

If applicable (within Procurement Quality), you will carry out supplier development and surveillance, supporting the qualification of supplier manufacturing processes and setting applicable requirements to suppliers.

If authorized, you will support the company's certifications and interfaces with external quality authorities (ISO, IAQG, EASA, etc.,).

KEY DELIVERABLES: Control and Inspection Plan, Product/Service Conformity for a given domain, Non Conformance Analysis and Action Plan, Supplier and Development Assurance Plan, APQP Key Business Deliverables (when owner).

KEY ACCOUNTABILITIES: to demonstrate conformity of processes and requirements for a given domain to Programme Quality Assurance and External Authorities (when authorized), to drive non conformity management at domain level for Programme Quality Assurance.

Titulo: Energy Laboratory Test Means Testing Engineer

Job Description:

Job Description:

Come Join Our Team!

Flight and Integration Test Center is growing. If you have the passion, ambition, energy and competence to make a step change in this transformation - we want you!. Your dream job starts here!.

If your profile matches with our requirements and you are interested in joining our dynamic and multicultural team of professionals, you can complete your application online (providing your CV and cover letter outlining your motivation).

Join Airbus now and embrace the opportunity to make a difference. We make it fly and you can too.

As part of Airbus, the job holder will be accountable for operation and maintenance of energy laboratory test means devoted to electrical / pneumatic / fuel / hydraulic / mechanical aircraft systems.

The job holder has direct accountability to Airbus Head of LTM Energy Lab Development & Operation.

Your Main Responsibilities:

Design electro-mechanical test means, including technologies to be applied, instrumentation, materials, etc, to fulfill requirements.

Design control and acquisition software for electro-mechanical test means, including technologies to be applied, instrumentation, safety alarms, etc, to fulfil requirements.

Design and commissioning test set-up facilities, including calibration of sensors/transducers.

Define test procedures to be executed on the test bench based on Laboratory Test Requirements delivered by Design Offices.

Implement test procedures ensuring proper test execution and consistency of data recorded.

Analyse test data ensuring proper pass/fail criteria of the unit/system under test.

Write laboratory test reports supporting validation and verification activities of the overall A/C system development.

Ensure test quality on external deliveries.

Maintain calibrated and inventoried material laboratory database.

Maintain industrial facilities test means ensuring laboratory availability.

Identify and implement proper corrective actions in case of malfunction of industrial test means.

Attend test campaign meetings (Kick-Off Meeting, follow-up meeting and troubleshooting for problem investigation and problem resolution).

Identify technical risks and opportunities for on-going activities and ensure the execution of adequate mitigation plans.

As our Frontend Engineer in Spain, you’ll join an established and growing engineering hub that has already become a strong part of our organisation. Our office there is no longer new. It is a stable and integrated setup with close collaboration across teams and locations.

You’ll play a key role in shaping the future of our product and platform, working with a modern tech stack to build robust, scalable features that create real impact. This is not about starting from scratch. It is about strengthening and further evolving a mature engineering environment, contributing to technical standards, and helping us continue to raise the bar together.

Tasks

What you’ll be doing

Your work will directly shape how thousands of users experience JOIN. As a Senior Frontend Engineer, you’ll take strong ownership of delivering high-impact features end-to-end.

What that looks like:

Own frontend feature delivery end-to-end – from technical discovery and solution design to implementation, testing, rollout, and monitoring in production.
Translate product requirements into scalable technical solutions, proactively identifying edge cases, UX trade-offs, and performance implications before implementation begins.
Build complex, maintainable React components and application flows using TypeScript and modern frontend architecture principles.
Drive technical quality within your scope by writing robust unit, and end-to-end tests and ensuring high reliability of shipped features.
Actively contribute to architectural discussions, proposing improvements to frontend structure, state management patterns, performance optimisation, and scalability.
Collaborate closely with Product and Design during discovery, challenging assumptions and ensuring solutions balance user experience, feasibility, and speed.
Monitor and optimise performance and user experience in production, using metrics, logs, and analytics to continuously improve feature impact.
Support incremental improvements to the Design System and shared components when feature development requires enhancements.
Mentor less experienced engineers through collaboration and knowledge sharing (without formal people management responsibilities).


Who you’ll work with

Our mission as Frontend Guild is to provide JOIN’s customers and their candidates with a smooth UX experience, simplified and beautiful UI, and rich functionality to achieve their goals by using our data-rich capabilities.
We strongly believe that Frontend engineering should be collaborative with product and design teams, that our UI is diverse and has accessibility and security in mind.
We take care of our guild’s tools by building a robust and Figma-synced Design System, code generators and coding conventions to create consistency, avoid wasting time on scaffolding and clutter, and focus on the business logic and customer value.
Stability is our utmost priority, hence we write E2E tests for all scenarios and optimize performance to make our product feel a bliss to use.

What we’re looking for

✔ 4+ years of experience as a Frontend Engineer working with modern JavaScript frameworks (React preferred).




✔ Strong understanding of TypeScript, HTML5, and CSS3.


✔ Solid experience with state management libraries (Redux, Zustand, or similar).


✔ Knowledge of API integration patterns and data fetching (REST, GraphQL).


✔ Familiarity with testing frameworks such as Jest, React Testing Library, or Cypress.


✔ Understanding of CI/CD pipelines and version control using Git.


✔ Experience working collaboratively in cross-functional Agile teams.


✔ Strong focus on performance, accessibility, and usability.

Bonus points for:

Experience contributing to or maintaining a Design System.
Familiarity with Figma and UI collaboration workflows.
Exposure to backend concepts or full-stack environments.
Experience with analytics or experimentation tools (e.g. Statsig, A/B testing).

We adapt quickly and embrace change in a growth-oriented startup environment
We continuously challenge ourselves and each other to improve

Who we are:
Want to make an impact? Join our pack and come work (and play!) with us.

We believe everyone deserves the unconditional love of a pet—and at Rover, our mission is to make it easier to experience that love. Founded in 2011, the Rover app and website connect dog and cat parents with loving pet sitters and dog walkers in neighborhoods across the US, Canada, and Europe. At Rover, our furry coworkers are just as important as our human ones—and we wouldn’t have it any other way. Along with making the joys of pet parenthood more accessible, we’re committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and welcoming community of pet people—and that starts with our employees.

Who We're Looking For

We're looking for a technically-minded Lead Analyst who loves the intersection of Data Modeling and Marketing Strategy. You are someone who isn't satisfied with just "pulling numbers"—you want to understand and architect the underlying logic that makes those numbers true.

In this role, you will be the technical backbone of the Marketing Analytics team. You will take ownership of our reporting infrastructure, driving the transition from legacy reports to clean, automated insights. You will act as the bridge between raw data and advanced Data Science, ensuring our data models are robust enough to support rigorous analytics and experimentation.

You will also serve as the subject matter expert for one of our most complex domains: Attribution. You will navigate our datasets to refine and maintain our logic—helping us distinguish between actions that lead to account creation versus those that drive repeat bookings—ensuring our Lifecycle and Acquisition stakeholders have a reliable view of their impact.

This is a hybrid position that has the minimum expectation to work out of our Barcelona Office in the Poblenou neighborhood two days per week on Mondays and Thursdays. For this reason, candidates must be based in Barcelona or willing to relocate.
Your Impact:
Own Marketing Attribution: Serve as the custodian of our attribution logic. You will maintain and refine our models to ensure the business understands how provider and owner strategies compound to drive New and Repeat bookings, constantly looking for ways to improve our measurement accuracy.
Architect the Reporting Suite: Tackle technical debt head-on by modernizing our reporting infrastructure. You will own the migration of critical business reports to our BI tools, designing clean, SQL-heavy calculations that serve as the "single source of truth" for Lifecycle Marketing and leadership.
Scale Experimentation Operations: Unblock the team on testing by operationalizing our experimentation framework. You will manage the transition to new tools (like Statsig), create SQL templates for test analysis, and conduct code reviews to ensure statistical rigor across the team.
Enable Advanced Analytics: Partner with our Data Scientists to perform Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA) and build robust data models. By ensuring our data foundation is solid, you will free up the team to focus on high-impact predictive modeling (LTV, Churn) and strategic deep dives.
Your Qualifications:
6+ years of experience in Analytics or Analytics Engineering, with a specific focus on Marketing or Growth data.
Expert-level SQL proficiency. You don't just write queries; you write performant, readable code for complex data modeling, window functions, and heavy calculations within BI tools.
Deep understanding of Marketing Attribution logic. You have experience building, interpreting, or maintaining models that track user journeys across multiple touchpoints (Acquisition vs. Retention/Repeat).
Experience with Data Modeling and creating "Gold Layer" datasets that allow other analysts and stakeholders to self-serve easily.
Strong knowledge of experimentation logic. You understand how to set up data structures to track control vs. variant groups and check for sample ratio mismatches.
Proven ability to manage technical projects from end-to-end, such as migrating reports between tools or auditing tracking setups.
Communication skills that allow you to translate complex data engineering concepts into benefits for non-technical marketing stakeholders.
Nice to Have:
Experience with code-first BI tools (e.g. Mode, Looker, Tableau) where SQL is the primary driver of analysis.
Experience with experimentation platforms like Statsig.
Familiarity with CRM tools (e.g., Iterable), Customer Data Platforms (CDPs), and how their data flows into a warehouse.
Background in a two-sided marketplace.

The Sales Development Representative (SDR) is responsible for generating and qualifying high-quality sales pipelines to support revenue growth.

● Sales Skills: Proven ability to conduct discovery calls, qualify opportunities, present solutions, and influence decision-makers; strong experience with outbound cold calling and multi-channel outreach.

● Soft Skills: Proactive, motivated, customer-oriented, and team player; excellent oral, written, presentation, and interpersonal communication; high emotional intelligence, adaptability, critical thinking, and solution-oriented mindset.

Job Description


We’re a profitable, scaled SaaS company with 2,000+ employees worldwide, expanding our pricing function. This is a rare opportunity to shape and scale a growing strategic function that has strong executive support and investment. You have the chance to define what excellence looks like.

To fuel our expansion and drive market success, we are seeking an experienced and dynamic global Pricing Manager.

This is a critical role with high visibility across the company and high impact on the business. You will create and execute global pricing and packaging strategies. You will understand key needs, perceptions and value drivers of EcoVadis prospects and customers, create pricing and packaging to meet needs, and work to execute across internal teams including executives, strategy, RevOps, methodology, product management, sales, sales enablement, customer support, marketing and other teams.

Key Responsibilities

Develop global pricing strategy, from analysis to launch:

Develop and execute the pricing strategy to maximize both short-term and long-term objectives. Including:
Track market trends (customer buying patterns, changing market conditions, leading macroeconomic indicators, competitive pricing, inflation) to inform approaches;
Work across internal teams to identify pricing needs and feedback;
Analyze growth and profitability metrics, and identify opportunities to improve pricing at the solution-level, package-level, feature-level and geography-level;
Work with Market Research to run market research to identify customer and prospect needs, gaps, pain points and perceptions in the area of pricing;
Share pricing insights on an ongoing basis and as needed with internal teams like executive leadership, strategy, product management, frontline, RevOps, and across marketing.

Test, launch, monitor and adjust pricing approaches:

Manage internal project teams across RevOps, Strategy, Solutions Consulting, Tech teams, Marketing and Product teams to deliver, test and launch approaches;
Provide recommendations on discount levels (across solutions, features, packages);
Build pricing documentation and objection-handling enablement for frontline teams;
Contribute to business plans, helping to set and monitor targets, revenue and profitability;
Analyze the ongoing impact of pricing changes (financial and other impact).

Since we’re a rapidly-growing business, you may contribute to other projects as needed.


Qualifications

Bachelor or masters degree in business, economics, science, or a related field.
6+ years relevant business experience in pricing, revenue optimization, finance or strategy in the B2B Tech or SaaS industries (highly preferred).
Experience analyzing, defining, recommending, testing and executing pricing and packaging strategies for a B2B SaaS company with a multi-product / solution portfolio.
Solid knowledge of pricing and packaging approaches and strategies, and can recommend the right approach to achieve different goals.
Solid experience in experimentation and piloting to test approaches.
Strong financial acumen and quantitative skills: data analysis, scenario and decision modeling, performance tracking using metrics and KPIs.
Commercial mindset - a focus on driving business growth and profitability by understanding customer needs, value drivers, and solutions.
Excellent skills distilling complex models into key insights and clear and actionable outputs and recommendations.
Comfortable creating and running multiple cross-functional projects from concept to actionable output. Solid experience giving clear guidance and effective communications with cross-functional teams.
Solid project management experience in a cross-functional environment. Highly collaborative (culturally sensitive, great listening, interaction, adaptability, stakeholder management).
Strategic thinking, and ability to effectively bring concepts to execution.
A self-starter that thrives in an entrepreneurial and highly collaborative environment.
Attention to detail, drive for quality, and ability to deliver results quickly.
English fluency required, other languages a plus.
[preferred] Knowledge of ESG initiatives and a passion for ESG progress.

Titulo: Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws specialist
Descripcion_Completa:
Job Description:

Join the Flight Physics Centre of Competence (CoC) at Airbus Defence & Space (AD&S) Engineering, a dynamic and inclusive German-Spanish organization dedicated to shaping the future of air power solutions. We are seeking a passionate and innovative Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Engineer to join our team and help us redefine the skies. At Airbus, we believe in the power of diversity and are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive.

The selected candidate will join the Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Team in Getafe to contribute to the development of the on-going projects (EF2000, FCAS, MRTT, SIRTAP, M&L;...)

Flight the Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Domain mission is to provide our customers safely flying air vehicles with the best possible performance, agility and handling/flying qualities by designing extraordinary control laws and performing comprehensive clearance assessments within the full flying envelope. Main task and responsibilities of Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws Domain are:

Definition of the flight control system requirements (aircraft stability requirements, aircraft control power requirement, sensors requirements, actuators requirements, electric and hydraulic flight control system requirements,...)
Design of the flight control laws algorithms thar provide system stability augmentation, command system, autopilots and automatic function to reduce pilot workload and ensure optimal Handling Qualities
Produce a comprehensive flight clearance that ensure a safe flight test envelope expansion, data gathering of required test data and demonstration of the desired performances of the aircraft flight control system
Produce aircraft simulation models, simulation environment build-up to allow the development, validation and verification of the flight control laws software design as well as flight test matching of the aircraft dynamic models for training simulators accreditation
Produce the documentation dossiers to grant product Handling Qualities certification and qualification
Contribute to flight control system development and certification that is a key complex system in all AIRBUS products that significantly contribute to our customer satisfaction

What you will do

Shape the Future: Contribute to current and future Flight Dynamics and Flight Control Laws projects, R&D, certification processes, and negotiations with certification authorities. Your insights will drive innovation and excellence.
Innovate and Inspire: Support the design phase of new programs and derivatives of existing products, evaluating the impact of configuration changes on aircraft handling qualities and mission effectiveness. Your creativity will be the catalyst for ground breaking solutions.
Customer-Centric Approach: Conduct handling qualities studies and flight control laws enhancements to support sales campaigns, RFPs, and customer inquiries. Provide operational tools and documentation for flight operations, and assist with flight data analysis for development, certification, training, and incident investigations. Your customer-focused mindset will ensure we deliver exceptional service.
Strategic Implementation: Develop and implement roadmaps for tool harmonization, data management, and marketing of simulation services. Drive the Flight Physics strategy and grow our service portfolio. Your strategic thinking will help us stay ahead of the curve.
Quality and Excellence: Ensure the highest standards in technical dossier content and presentation. Implement digitalization, capability, and technology roadmaps while ensuring R&T, R&D, and program delivery. Your attention to detail will set the bar for excellence.
Collaborate and Lead: Contribute to team and program activities, fostering synergies and continuous improvement. Communicate effectively with internal and external stakeholders, demonstrating strong problem-solving and organizational skills. Your leadership will inspire and motivate the team.
Customer Focus: Inspire confidence and credibility with internal and external customers, anticipating their needs and delivering exceptional service. Your customer-centric approach will build lasting relationships.

RESPONSIBILITIES/MAIN TASKS

The jobholder will be responsible of the following task:

Perform the stability analysis of aircraft relevant configuration in failure free and under degraded system states
Analyze nonlinear aircraft simulation in failure free and under degraded system states to ensure adequate aircraft response, departure resistance and adequate handling qualities’
Maintain and upgrade simulation models, pre and post processing tools to perform above mentioned tasks
Perform trade-off to identify root cause of the deficiencies found during the flight clearance activities
Organize flight dynamic manned simulation activities required to answer programs needs during a flight clearance loop
Define the flight test campaign required for simulation models data gathering and for Handling Qualities assessment
Propose flight control laws updates based on clearance and flight test results
Flight test analysis to update/validate simulation models and for the preparation of handling qualities certification/qualification dossier

SKILLS

We are looking for candidates with the following skills and experience:

Educated in an Aeronautical Engineering degree (MASTER in air vehicles)
Proficiency in English at a negotiation level. German knowledge will be valued
Knowledge of FORTRAN, C, Matlab, Simulink
Experience in flight dynamics activities will be consider as a plus. Your expertise will be invaluable to our team.
Passion for Improvement: A commitment to continuous improvement and a willingness to roll up your sleeves to get the job done. Your dedication will drive our success.
Strategic Thinking: The ability to think strategically and execute methodically. Your vision will guide our projects to new heights.
Resilience: The capability to work under pressure and travel as required. Your adaptability will be key to our success.
Integrity: A strong awareness of compliance risks and a commitment to acting with integrity. Your ethical standards will uphold our reputation.
Capability to integrate in a multi-cultural team
High adaptability to work on new topics, new business and in a network mode *competences

At Airbus Defence & Space, we are committed to fostering an inclusive culture where everyone can reach their full potential. We encourage women to apply and be part of a team that is transforming the future of aviation. Together, we can achieve greatness and make a lasting impact on the world.

Titulo: Airworthiness Technical Engineer for Instructions for Continued Airworthiness (ICAs) and Generic and Individual Repairs.
Descripcion_Completa:
Job Description:

Mission:

The main mission of the Airworthiness Technical Engineer for Instructions for Continued Airworthiness (ICAs) and Generic and Individual Repairs is to define applicable certification requirements for the ALS and other ICA manuals(AMM, Trouble Shooting Manual, IPC, etc..) to demonstrate its compliance. Airworthiness Technical Engineers are also expected to participate in Generic and Individual repairs approval as part of the different aircraft manuals and continuing airworthiness artefacts.

Functions and Tasks:

Define applicable certification requirements for ICAs (AMM, Trouble Shooting Manual, IPC, etc..), including ALS and demonstrate its compliance.
Coordinate and perform the airworthiness approval of Individual and Generic Repairs, exercising DOA/MDOA privileges (when available) as delegated by the Chief of the Office of Airworthiness.
Provide expertise in ICAs and other continuing airworthiness artefacts to the design organization.
Contribute to the definition of policies for the discipline under his/her scope, and promote their consistent application across programmes, including the participation in the development of new and foreseen airworthiness strategies or policies, in particular for novel or unusual design features or for compliance demonstration method improvement.
Contribute to Airbus DS Research and Technology (R&T) projects related to the discipline (repairs using additive manufacturing, maintenance of future, etc..).
Contribute to develop specialized training to the overall Design Organization on the discipline under his/her scope.
Support the timely achievement approval of ICAs, Individual and Generic Repairs through identification of technical issues and associated risk management plan, identification of regulation and interpretation issues. All of this in coordination with design office, including relevant CVEs, Chief Engineer and Services Organisation.

Required Skills:

Managing trans-functional and trans-national projects.
More than 5 years of experience in the described functions.
Master in Aeronautical Engineering
Proven experience in ICAs and ALS manual and/or source data generation.
Experience in Airworthiness functions, in particular in structure (static and fatigue) and/or systems certification.
Experience interfacing with the Aviation Authorities, will be a plus for the position.
Knowledge about repair process and other ICA is desirable.
Fluent English (knowledge of Spanish language will be a plus for the position).
Being capable to develop a CVE/DAE role in a short period of time after recruitment.

About Overwatch Mission Critical

Overwatch is a service-disabled Veteran-owned small business (SDVOB) certified through the National Veterans Business Development Council (NVBDC), offering construction professional services, talent acquisition, and general contractor services for the mission-critical infrastructure industry.

Our mission is the construction and management of state-of-the-art data centers with the precision and reliability this industry demands. From high-end engineers to seasoned professionals, we deploy the people you need to get your data center off the ground. At Overwatch, it's more than a job — it's purpose.

The Opportunity

Overwatch Mission Critical is building out this team to support multiple concurrent projects.

This role will operateduring the initial design and preconstruction phases, with heavy collaboration across design, engineering, and project controls teams. As the project transitions into active construction, you will be consistently expected to travel and be present on-site to oversee build execution, drive technical coordination, and support turnover through project closeout.

As a Data Center Construction Owner’s Representative, you will lead the end-to-end delivery of large-scale, technically complex data center construction projects. Acting as a trusted extension of the Owner, you will be responsible for aligning design, engineering, and construction teams to ensure on-time, on-budget, and high-quality project delivery. This is a high-impact role requiring strategic leadership, technical expertise, and direct engagement with both internal stakeholders and external vendor partners.

The successful candidate will demonstrate the ability to own concurrent builds from initial planning through turnover to operations, while maintaining accountability for cost, schedule, quality, and risk management.

Key Responsibilities

Serve as the end-to-end program leader, driving delivery from concept through completion.
Project Planning: Develop and implement strategies for data center construction that align with client goals, timelines, and budgets.
Design & Construction Coordination: Partner with design and construction teams to ensure seamless handoff, technical alignment, and proactive resolution of constructability issues.
Tenant Fit-Out & LV Integration: Manage coordination of tenant scope and low-voltage infrastructure with base build delivery.
Project Management: Oversee all phases of construction, ensuring milestones are achieved with precision and accountability.
Vendor & Contractor Management: Select, negotiate, and manage contractors, consultants, and suppliers, ensuring adherence to scope, safety, quality, and cost.
Budget Oversight: Build, manage, and optimize project budgets with strong financial controls.
Risk Management: Identify and mitigate risks to protect schedule, quality, and financial performance.
Stakeholder Engagement: Communicate effectively with client leadership, internal teams, and external partners, providing transparent reporting and proactive issue resolution.
Quality Assurance: Implement and enforce quality control processes to meet stringent mission-critical construction standards.
Conduct regular project site visits to verify progress, safety, and quality throughout project lifecycle.

Required Skills & Experience

Project Management Expertise: Proven track record of successfully delivering large-scale, mission-critical construction projects (data center experience strongly preferred).
Strategic Planning: Skilled in developing and executing long-term program strategies across multiple concurrent builds.
Technical Knowledge: Comprehensive understanding of data center design, construction, commissioning, and operational requirements.
Leadership: Strong leadership under pressure with the ability to inspire, mentor, and drive diverse project teams toward success.
Budget Management: Demonstrated experience developing, tracking, and optimizing large project budgets.
Risk Management: Proficient in identifying potential risks and implementing effective mitigation strategies.
Vendor Management: Expertise in contract negotiation, vendor oversight, and maintaining accountability among partners.
Communication: Exceptional verbal and written communication skills for client-facing updates, executive reporting, and contractor coordination.
Bilingual in Spanish or Portuguese is a plus.
Problem-Solving: Strong analytical and decision-making skills with the ability to excel in fast-paced, high-stakes environments.

Physical Requirements and Work Environment

This position operates primarily in an active data center construction environment, encompassing both indoor and outdoor work settings. The role requires the physical ability to perform essential job functions safely and effectively in a dynamic environment that includes ongoing construction activity and evolving site conditions.

The work environment includes areas and temporary structures owned, leased, or controlled by third parties over which Overwatch may have limited or no control. Because the project remains under construction, certain areas of the site — including access routes, staging zones, and temporary structures — may not yet be fully ADA-compliant until construction is complete or those third-party areas are finalized.

Physical Requirements:

Must be able to walk, stand, stoop, twist, bend, and climb stairs or ladders for extended periods while navigating uneven, unpaved, or obstructed terrain.
Must be able to lift, carry, push, or pull up to 50 pounds on an occasional basis.
Must be able to drive between job sites and access all areas of an active construction zone, including raised platforms, scaffolding, and confined spaces.
Must be able to tolerate exposure to outdoor weather conditions, dust, and construction-related noise, vibrations, and odors.
Must be able to communicate effectively with contractors, engineers, and site personnel, and maintain situational awareness in high-activity environments.
Personal protective equipment (PPE), including hard hats, safety vests, hearing protection, and steel-toe boots, is required.
Must have sufficient visual acuity to read drawings, specifications, and safety signage, and to observe site activity.

Work Environment:

Work is performed primarily in and around active construction areas, which may include unfinished buildings, temporary field offices, staging areas, and partially completed infrastructure. These locations may contain uneven surfaces, limited accessibility, and other conditions typical of construction projects prior to final completion. Overwatch does not control and cannot modify accessibility conditions within areas, facilities, or temporary structures owned or managed by third parties.﻿

Reasonable Accommodation:

Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable qualified individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions of this position, provided such accommodations do not create safety hazards, impede essential mobility within active work zones, or require modification of facilities not owned or controlled by Overwatch.

Descripcion_Completa:
PharmEng/Efor is a leading pharmaceutical and biopharmaceutical consulting firm committed to providing innovative solutions to the challenges facing our industries. We pride ourselves on our dedication to quality, safety, and excellence in everything we do. As we continue to grow, we are seeking a highly qualified and motivated Data Architect to join our team.

Job Title: Data Architect Operational Analytics & MES Integration

Location: Spain and France

Position Overview:

We are seeking a hands-on, business-savvy Data Architect to lead the design, development, and ongoing management of data systems that support daily manufacturing operations and performance analytics. This role blends strategic data architecture with daily tactical supportensuring clean, connected, and contextualized data from MES (Rockwell PharmaSuite), SQL databases, and Power BI to empower decision-makers across operations, quality, and leadership teams.

Key Responsibilities:

Data Architecture & Integration (Strategic):

Design and implement a structured, scalable data architecture linking MES, ERP, LIMS, and SCADA systems.
Build and manage SQL-based data pipelines and ETL processes to support manufacturing analytics and compliance reporting.
Establish data standards, definitions, and governance protocols to ensure high data quality and consistency.

Analytics & Daily Operations Support:

Develop and maintain Power BI dashboards that provide real-time insights into production metrics (e.g., batch cycle times, downtime, OEE, quality deviations).
Serve as the day-to-day go-to for operations, QA, and engineering teams for data troubleshooting, ad hoc reporting, and system data queries.
Collaborate with floor personnel to translate real-world process challenges into data-driven solutions.
Monitor daily data feeds and alerts from MES and other systems to proactively address data gaps or process disruptions.

Process Optimization & Continuous Improvement:

Identify inefficiencies or blind spots in current data workflows and lead efforts to resolve them with better automation, analytics, or data visibility.
Support and participate in Lean, Six Sigma, or continuous improvement initiatives with data analysis and visualization.
Conduct root cause data investigations and recommend system or process changes.

Qualifications:

Bachelors or Masters degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, or related field.
4+ years of hands-on experience in data analytics, preferably within manufacturing, life sciences, or pharmaceuticals.
Proficient in SQL (views, stored procedures, query tuning) and relational database management (e.g., SQL Server).
Proven experience with Power BI (data modeling, DAX, dashboard design, publishing).
Experience working with Rockwell PharmaSuite or other MES platforms (functional knowledge sufficient for integrating/reporting).
Ability to engage with cross-functional stakeholdersoperators to senior leadershipand translate data into actionable insights.

Preferred Skills:

Familiarity with GxP, cGMP, and FDA data integrity requirements.
Understanding of ISA-95 hierarchy and MES-to-ERP integration.
Experience with scripting or automation tools (e.g., Python, Power Automate) a plus.
Comfortable in fast-paced, hands-on environments where priorities shift based on operational needs.

Join Us: If you are a dedicated and highly motivated individual with a passion for ensuring the highest standards of Engineering and Quality in the Pharmaceutical Industry, we encourage you to apply. PharmEng/Efor offers a dynamic and inclusive work environment, competitive compensation, and opportunities for professional growth. Join our team and contribute to the success of our industry-leading solutions.

PharmEng/Efor is an equal opportunity employer and welcomes candidates of all backgrounds to apply.

About us

Your new role

Digital Science is seeking a Principal Product Manager (B2C) to drive revenue and product growth across our industry-leading B2C product suite. This includes established products like Overleaf as well as new AI-driven and mobile app initiatives.

In this role, you will collaborate closely with product development, go-to-market, and executive teams to shape, prioritise, and deliver high-impact product initiatives that enhance user experience and drive business outcomes. You'll be responsible for defining strategy, identifying growth opportunities, and ensuring our B2C offerings continue to serve our customers.

Reporting to the Product Director (Overleaf), you will work closely with Executive and Senior Vice Presidents, alongside functional leaders from Analytics and Design across our matrix organisation. To succeed, you will need to develop a deep understanding of Digital Science’s entire B2C product ecosystem—both from a commercial, technology and product perspective.

Please note - due to business need, we can only accept applications from candidates who reside in the following locations where we have established legal entities: UK, Spain, Germany and Romania.

If you apply from outside of these areas, your application will not be considered.

Please be aware that we may close this position early if we receive a high volume of applications, so we encourage you to apply promptly.


What you’ll be doing



Product success
You will primarily be responsible for driving our B2C growth strategy. You will own our B2C growth framework and will be responsible for all aspects of experimentation.
You will identify and execute experiments that increase user acquisition, activation, retention and monetisation across the lifecycle of our B2C product suite.
You will work closely with go-to-market teams to deliver high-impact product lead growth initiatives. This includes but is not limited to improving onboarding experiences and optimising paid conversion funnels.
Expand AI and mobile offerings. Identify and develop new opportunities in AI-driven tools and mobile apps to enhance our B2C portfolio and drive long-term business success.
Product management
Be a trusted product expert, consistently applying your deep knowledge of the business, customers, and technology to your product work.
Provide visibility to and inspire confidence in all stakeholders, both external and internal, with respect to our product roadmap and development plans.
Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure product success and regularly report on product performance to stakeholders.
Have and apply a solid and pragmatic understanding of common Product Management practices, determining and applying the best framework or methodology for any given situation.
Guide or mentor others. This role does not include line management, but we would like you to pass on your knowledge and experience to the rest of the team.
Vision, strategy, and prioritisation
Guide the long-term strategy, vision, and roadmap for your product area, ensuring they directly contribute to revenue growth, customer satisfaction, and competitive advantage.
Leverage insights from data analysis to identify opportunities for innovation and inform product strategy.
Assess, manage, and communicate the prioritisation of features and initiatives, using data, insights, and strategic alignment to determine their relative value, viability, and urgency.
Monitor product performance, using data analytics, metrics, and user feedback to identify areas for optimization and make data-driven decisions.
Maintain an awareness of the competitive B2C landscape by undertaking research to understand how other consumer products implement effective growth strategies.
Product development and practice
Lead the end-to-end product development lifecycle, from ideation to launch and later to end-of-life, ensuring timely delivery and high-quality execution.
Manage product ideation within your product areas (including idea generation, analysis, and documentation), continuously looking for the best way to solve business problems and improve user experience.
Specify and validate requirements required for comprehensive product development, including the creation and maintenance of high-quality product artefacts such as PRDs, epics, and user stories.
Own validation of development work ensuring delivery of value to the customer and business.
Facilitate collaboration between members of our cross-functional product teams.

You will have
At least 5 years experience in Product Management working on B2C consumer technology products as a Senior or Principal Product Manager.
Experience driving product strategy across a product portfolio in a matrix organisation.
High numeracy with experience in quantitative techniques, such as A/B and MVT testing.
A proven track record of working in high performing product growth teams with a demonstrable track record of success.
Demonstrated ability to figure out solutions to hard problems with many constraints, using sound judgement to assess risks, and deliver solutions.
Proven ability to foster cross-functional collaboration among teams with diverse areas of expertise and responsibility, leveraging product management tools and frameworks to drive alignment and deliver results.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills including strong presentation and relationship management skills.
Highly developed organisational skills and ability to work with a high level of autonomy.
Demonstrated ability to adapt projects in response to changing requirements.
A passion for staying up-to-date with industry trends and emerging technologies.
Additional highly desirable, but not essential criteria
Experience in developing products used by researchers and scientists.

HiveMQ is the Industrial AI Platform helping enterprises move from connected devices to intelligent operations. Built on the MQTT standard and a distributed edge-to-cloud architecture, HiveMQ connects and governs industrial data in real time, enabling global leaders like Audi, BMW, Eli Lilly, and Siemens to operationalize AI and drive innovation at scale.

At HiveMQ, our culture is Effortless Empowered Relentless. We make the complex simple, act with confidence and ownership, and never stop pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Join us to power the future of intelligent industry.

HiveMQ’s Vision for this role

As VP of Product Management, you will own the execution, performance, and long‑term success of HiveMQ’s product portfolio across data streaming, data intelligence, and agentic Acting as the Chief Product Officer’s right hand, you will define HiveMQ’s product development philosophy and design and run an AI‑first product development operating model at scale, aligning Product, Design, and Engineering into one cohesive system that delivers tangible customer and revenue impact. You bring a senior product leadership profile and a player–coach mindset, able to be hands-on in the early stages while building the leaders and systems that drive outcomes through organizational scale and leverage.

This role requires a proven track record of taking products from 0-to-1 successfully - you've done before in the data and analytics domain, and the products you've launched have demonstrably succeeded in the market.


Who are you?

The ideal candidate for this job should have most of the following:

Extensive product leadership experience – typically 15+ years of total work experience, with a significant portion in product management, and 7+ years leading managers and distributed teams across geographies and time zones.

Proven business ownership for a major product line or business unit, including setting multi‑year product strategy, driving growth and retention, and owning key revenue and efficiency KPIs (you are comfortable “owning the number” from the product side).

Demonstrated 0-to-1 product success, including taking at least one (ideally two) products from concept to market success in the data and analytics domain.

Strong ability to design and run a scalable product operating model, aligning Product, Design, and Engineering, and establishing AI‑first, evidence‑based decision making as the default way of working.

Strong financial and portfolio acumen: you treat headcount, budget, and engineering capacity as capital to deploy; you are skilled at making clear tradeoffs on where to invest, where to accelerate, and where to stop.

Track record of building and leading high‑performing leadership teams (Directors and Senior Directors), including hiring, coaching, and evolving the product management organization to match changing strategic priorities.

You’re an experienced product leader who has already run a major product line or business and has the presence and drive to lead an entire function, not just a single product.

A true player–coach mentality: you are comfortable being hands‑on, initially leading a small Product team, and doing the work yourself rather than only managing through layers of people.

Strong domain alignment with data intelligence / analytics products similar to HiveMQ Pulse – you have helped customers turn streaming or industrial data into governed, contextualized, AI‑ready intelligence.

High cross‑regional collaboration comfort, able to work effectively across US and EU time zones with engineering, design, and GTM stakeholders.

A proven ability to create strong alignment between Product, Design, and Engineering, embedding modern, AI‑first product practices that connect clear strategy with disciplined execution through leaders and teams.


You might also have:

Deep domain experience in industrial software, IoT/IIoT, data platforms, analytics, or AI/ML products, ideally in enterprise or mission‑critical contexts.

Prior experience operating at VP/CPO level in a high‑growth B2B software or SaaS company, with direct responsibility for P&L, pricing and packaging, and tight GTM alignment.

A strong track record of leading AI‑native transformations – embedding AI both into product capabilities (e.g., data intelligence, automation, decisioning) and into product development workflows (discovery, roadmap, analytics, portfolio management).

Experience rebuilding or scaling a product management function, including defining org structure, hiring strategy, career paths, and PM practices for a modern, outcome‑driven organization.

Recognized thought leadership in product management (e.g., speaking, writing, or community leadership) and comfort representing product strategy with customers, partners, and occasionally board‑level stakeholders.


You will:

Take over leadership of the Product function, initially managing a small Product and Docs team, and then shaping and building the broader product organization to support HiveMQ’s Industrial AI strategy.

Define the multi‑year product vision and strategy for HiveMQ’s Industrial AI platform, translating company goals into clear portfolio choices, investments, and bets across data streaming, data intelligence, and agentic AI.

Lead 0-to-1 product initiatives from conception through market success, leveraging your proven experience launching new data intelligence and analytics products.

Design and run the product development operating model at scale, including planning and portfolio processes, discovery and experimentation standards, product metrics, and AI‑first, data‑driven decision practices.

Align Product, Design, and Engineering into one cohesive system, ensuring strong partnerships, clear ownership, and an operating cadence that lets teams execute autonomously while staying tightly connected to strategy.

Work in a tight Product–Design–Engineering leadership triangle with the CTO and VP Engineering, ensuring end‑to‑end alignment from architecture to user experience and customer outcomes.

Own product‑line business performance – adoption, ARR, retention, customer lifetime value, and efficiency – and make clear investment calls on where to double‑down, accelerate, or stop when outcomes aren’t there.

Build, lead, and develop a high‑performing leadership bench (Directors and Senior Directors), coaching them to independently run their domains, hire and grow top PM talent, and raise the bar for product craft and decision quality.

Partner closely with C‑suite peers to connect product strategy with revenue models, pricing and packaging, GTM motions, and strategic customer and partner plays.

Represent HiveMQ’s product strategy externally, building deep relationships with strategic customers and partners, managing critical customer escalations in partnership with Sales and CS/Support, and confidently articulating our roadmap and value.

Invest in shared platforms, automation, and AI‑assisted workflows that materially increase the productivity and leverage of Product, Design, and Engineering, enabling the organization to scale output faster than headcount growth.

About Refinery89

Refinery89 is a Dutch AdTech company headquartered in Madrid, working with premium publishers worldwide to optimize programmatic advertising performance. Through advanced, data-driven technology, Refinery89 helps publishers increase revenue, improve efficiency, and gain greater transparency across their monetization stack.

The company operates with an international, highly specialized team focused on delivering measurable results, operational excellence, and long-term partnerships in an evolving digital advertising ecosystem.

About the Role

As an Account Manager – Publishers (Brazil), you will manage and grow relationships with premium Portuguese-speaking publishers, acting as their main point of contact and trusted advisor. Your focus is to ensure strong performance, high satisfaction, and long-term retention by combining relationship management, performance optimization, and proactive support.

You will work closely with internal teams—including Publisher Sales, Technical Account Managers, AdTech, Product, and Finance—to deliver measurable outcomes while maintaining a consistently high-quality publisher experience.

Key Responsibilities

1. Account Ownership & Growth


Manage and grow relationships with premium publisher accounts in Brazil via email, WhatsApp, and calls.
Act as the primary point of contact, ensuring clarity, trust, and continuity.
2. Performance Management


Monitor, analyze, and interpret performance metrics to proactively identify optimization and growth opportunities.
Translate performance data into clear, actionable insights and recommendations.
3. Issue Resolution & Support


Take full ownership of publisher issues end-to-end.
Coordinate internally with relevant teams while remaining accountable for resolution and communication.
4. Strategic Advisory


Develop a strong understanding of the publisher monetization ecosystem and platform technology.
Identify upsell and expansion opportunities aligned with publisher performance and needs.
Act as a trusted advisor by providing data-driven guidance and strategic recommendations.
5. Internal Collaboration


Share publisher feedback and market insights with internal teams to improve products, workflows, and execution.
Work cross-functionally to support initiatives that drive publisher success.
6. Relationship Cadence & Communication


Conduct Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) and Annual Business Reviews (ABRs) with key partners.
Maintain high standards of communication, including fast response times, proactive updates, and clear closure of all issues.
Publisher Success Standards


Acknowledge messages or tickets as soon as possible.
Own issues end-to-end and escalate internally when required.
Proactively update publishers before they follow up.
Communicate clearly, empathetically, and transparently.
Always close the loop once issues are resolved.
Must-have Skills & Experience


Native Brazilian Portuguese (mandatory); medium to high proficiency in English.
2–3 years of experience in AdTech, digital advertising, or publishing.
Minimum 1 year of hands-on experience setting up campaigns in ad servers (e.g. Google Ad Manager).
Strong relationship management skills with senior stakeholders.
Ability to explain technical concepts in a simple, actionable way.
Data-driven mindset with experience using dashboards and performance reports.
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and proactive.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced, evolving environment.
Preferred Background


Experience working directly with publishers or monetization platforms.
AdTech background is a strong plus.
Interview Process

The interview process for this role consists of four stages:
1. Initial screening – Introductory call with a recruiter.
2. First interview – Video call with members of the Publisher Success team.
3. Second interview – Video call with the Hiring Manager and/or senior stakeholders.
4. Final interview – In-person meeting with the Hiring Manager and key team members.

Each stage is designed to assess role alignment, collaboration style, and long-term fit, while giving clear visibility into how Refinery89 operates and what success looks like in the role.
