Job Summary

Chirp is building a new kind of creator engagement platform—starting with a link-in-bio tool and evolving into a full-featured, consumer-facing experience across web, mobile, and eventually connected TV. Our vision is to help creators drive higher engagement and monetize more effectively by combining interactive links, personalized content, and real-time cross-platform updates into a single, dynamic fan destination.

As Product Manager, you’ll be responsible for defining and delivering this end-to-end product experience. Your mission is to turn Chirp into the most effective way for creators to drive clicks, grow their audience, and generate new revenue from their presence. You’ll work across surfaces and teams to ship intuitive, data-powered features that deliver value for both creators and their fans.

This role requires product intuition, a deep understanding of creator workflows, and the ability to lead execution from 02. If you want to own a product that combines personalization, monetization, social tools, and real-time data, we’d love to talk.


Key Responsibilities
Product Strategy & Vision
Define and evolve Chirp’s core product vision: from a smart link-in-bio tool to a full creator-owned engagement and monetization layer.
Lead the roadmap across platforms (web, mobile, connected TV), prioritizing scalable impact for both creators and their fans.
Translate product vision into concrete feature bets, tied to creator goals (CTR, revenue, engagement).
Discovery & Validation
Partner with creators and fans to validate problems, workflows, and unmet needs.
Use interviews, prototypes, and rapid testing to guide what we build (and what we don't).
Understand how different creator verticals (fashion, food, music, etc.) influence product structure and monetization models.
Interactive Experience Design
Work with Design and Engineering to create intuitive, beautiful tools for building and customizing creator pages.
Optimize for measurable engagement: improve CTR, session depth, and fan interaction.
Build features that support media embedding, dynamic offers, personalization, and multi-platform integration.
Monetization & Data Activation
Create systems for outbound monetization: rev-share via sponsored offers, real-time auction demand, and targeting by creator vertical and audience demographic.
Integrate audience data, link performance, and engagement metrics into creator-facing dashboards.
Build and optimize monetization surfaces that align with fan intent and brand demand.
Cross-Functional Execution & Launch
Collaborate with Growth, Engineering, Creator Partnerships, and Marketing to ensure successful rollout and adoption.
Define clear success metrics and share learnings from launches regularly.
Ensure go-to-market includes onboarding, retention hooks, and feedback loops.


Required Skills & Experience
Must-Haves
5+ years of product management experience, ideally in creator tools, social platforms, or consumer SaaS.
Experience shipping interactive, user-facing products across web and mobile.
Strong understanding of product discovery and hypothesis-driven development.
Familiarity with link-in-bio tools, UGC platforms, or creator monetization systems.
Strong communication and stakeholder alignment skills across engineering, design, and marketing.
Nice-to-Haves
Experience working with demand partners, auctions, or ad networks.
Exposure to creator verticals or having worked closely with creators directly.
Experience with analytics tools and A/B testing frameworks.
Who You Are
A builder: you love shaping v1 and iterating fast.
Outcome-focused: you care more about results than rituals.
Strategic and tactical: you can see around corners and ship this week.
User-first: you speak to creators and fans often and base decisions on their behavior.
Data-aware: you use metrics to guide, but not replace, good judgment.

We value speed, transparency, humility, and product excellence. You’ll work with a team that believes product success comes from tight feedback loops, smart bets, and a lot of learning along the way.

Report errors and propose solutions.
Write semantic rules to correct errors.
Manage the support tickets in Jira.

Interest in technology.

Titulo: Data Scientist Analyst/Consultant for Supply Chain
Descripcion_Completa:
About Accenture Data & AI for Supply Chain Team

We are a global Center of Excellence specializing in Data & AI for Supply Chain, with a rapidly growing team of 180+ professionals. Our mission is to turn data into a strategic advantage, driving operational excellence, agility, and smarter, faster decision-making across the end-to-end supply chain.

As we continue to expand, we are looking for passionate professionals to join our team and shape the future of intelligent supply chains.

Our Focus Areas

Supply Chain Planning – AI-driven demand forecasting, inventory optimization, and planning solutions to enhance agility and efficiency.

Fulfillment & Network Optimization – Advanced analytics to optimize logistics, warehouse management, distribution, network and last-mile delivery, improving speed, cost, and service levels.
Manufacturing – AI, machine learning, and IoT to drive predictive maintenance, production efficiency, and quality control in smart manufacturing.


Your Role

As a Data Scientist in Accenture’s Data & AI for Supply Chain -

Apply advanced analytics and AI techniques (e.g., optimization, simulation, machine learning) to solve complex fulfillment challenges across industries.

Translate industry-specific challenges into mathematical formulations or conceptual models and develop algorithms to drive optimal decisions.

Design, build, and continuously improve solutions :

Route & Flow Path Optimization – Improve delivery speed and reduce transportation costs.

Network Design & Center of Gravity Analysis – Optimize facility locations and distribution networks.

Workforce Scheduling & Resource Allocation – Maximize labor efficiency and service levels.

Order-to-Cash optimization – Optimize service strategies and stock allocation to customer

Digital Twins for Fulfillment – Create virtual models of logistics systems to simulate and test scenarios.

Work with cross-functional teams (engineers, consultants, data scientists) to deliver impactful solutions

Contribute to business development by shaping innovative offerings




Basic Qualifications:

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Mathematics, Statistics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.

Proficiency in English (additional languages are a plus).

2+ years of experience in Data & AI or related fields (we are hiring at different experience levels).


We seek professionals with strong expertise in AI, analytics, and optimization applied to supply chain challenges. While you don’t need to master all of them, experience in multiple areas will be highly valuable. Key skills include:

Programming: Python, R, SQL

Operation Reasearch & Simulation: LP, ILP, Heuristics/Meta-Heuristics, Discrete Events Simulation, digital twins.

Machine Learning & AI: Forecasting, predictive modeling, optimization algorithms.

Generative AI: LLMs, AI-driven scenario generation, synthetic data modeling, and workflow automation.

Cloud & Big Data: Experience with Azure, AWS, or GCP, including AI/ML services.

Supply Chain Solutions: Familiarity with o9, Blue Yonder (BY), SAP IBP, or similar tools.

Data Engineering & Visualization: Large-scale data processing, market solutions/platforms (Databricks, Snowflake, ..), and tools like Power BI, Tableau, or Looker.


Soft & Strategic Skills

Beyond technical expertise, we look for professionals who can think strategically, collaborate effectively, and drive impact in complex environments. Key skills include:

Problem-Solving & Critical Thinking: Ability to analyze supply chain challenges and design AI-driven solutions.

Business Acumen: Understanding the impact of AI and analytics on supply chain efficiency, resilience, and cost reduction.

Communication: Ability to translate complex data insights into actionable business recommendations for technical and non-technical audiences.

Agility & Innovation: Comfortable working in fast-paced, evolving environments, with a passion for continuous learning and innovation.

What’s in It for You?

Join Accenture’s Data & AI for Supply Chain team and unlock a challenging and rewarding career, where you will:

Work on high-impact AI & analytics projects with global clients.

Collaborate with a team of 180+ data scientists and engineers, including 20% PhDs in Mathematics, Computer Science, Physics, or Statistics.

Drive cutting-edge AI innovation, including Generative AI applications in supply chain.

Enjoy a flexible, hybrid work model in a Great Place to Work®-recognized company.

The position is based in Barcelona or Madrid in a hybrid work model, combining remote work with in-office collaboration to foster team synergy. Candidates must reside in Spain and have legal work authorization.

At Accenture, recognized as a Great Place To Work®, we embrace a hybrid work model that leverages technology and our facilities to maintain essential human connection with our teams and clients. This connection supports our culture of inclusion and diversity, making us, according to Refinitiv, the most diverse company in the world.

We also offer benefits such as:

Health, life, and accident insurance

Medical services and wellness programs

Flexible compensation and stock purchase plans

Flexibility programs (working hours, time off, vacation)

Personalized training paths

Sustainability initiatives and Accenture Foundation programs

Employee diversity networks

Additional benefits: Bankinter office with special conditions and profit sharing.

If you are excited about leading AI-driven transformations in supply chain, we’d love to hear from you!

Role Description:

Localisation Lead - French - Marketplace


About Us


At Booking.com, data drives our decisions. Technology is at our core. And innovation is everywhere. But our company is more than datasets, lines of code or A/B tests. We’re the thrill of the first night in a new place. The excitement of the next morning. The friends you encounter. The journeys you take. The sights you see. And the memories you make. Through our products, partners and people, we make it easier for everyone to experience the world.


Team description

The Localization team at Booking.com ensures that the company’s products, content, and customer experiences are tailored to global audiences. They develop and implement localization strategy, manage relationships with translation vendors, set and uphold rigorous quality standards, and collaborate with internal partners like Marketing and Product. The team leverages automation and internal/external expertise to deliver culturally relevant, high-quality translations at scale across multiple languages and regions. Their work enables Booking.com to connect with users worldwide, drive engagement, and support strategic initiatives like the Connected Trip, all while maintaining consistency, accuracy, and effectiveness across diverse markets.


Role Overview

The Localization Lead is responsible for production, delivery and quality of localization for their market and ensures all localization requirements are delivered on time, within budget and in line with local market needs. They help ensure that our platform and channels are culturally resonant, brand-aligned and market-optimized.


In this role, the Localization Lead oversees the quality of their language for all streams of localization, working closely with the central Quality Team. They will monitor and optimise language quality, and identify and escalate systemic issues affecting quality. They will also oversee product testing for their market conducted by third party vendors.


The Localization Lead acts as the ambassador for localization in the market(s), proactively building relationships with key stakeholders. They will also look for opportunities for market-specific localization strategy - as well as informing central localization strategy - to deliver a more locally-relevant and customer-centric experience.


Key Job Responsibilities and Duties

Delivery

Ensure on-time delivery of localization work for their language(s)/market(s)

Quality Monitoring:

Monitor quality of localized content for their market, provide feedback to quality vendors, address issues with translation vendors, take a lead role in arbitrations, and escalate recurring issues to the central Quality Team as required;

Oversee localization product testing for their market;

Manage localization assets (Localisation Style Guides, Glossaries, Translation Memories);

Vendor Management:

Assess and onboard new vendors;

Build and foster rapport with vendor linguists;

Localization services:

Provide urgent ad-hoc marketing translations as required;

Provide consultation on selected localisation services such as transcreation, voice-over, post-editing, etc.;

Strategy

Provide local market perspective to inform central localization strategy as required;

Identify localization opportunities in customer journey to enhance international customer experience for their market;

Be ambassador for localization and establish relationships with key localization stakeholders in the market;

Provide cultural, linguistic, and market consultation to the business as required;

Facilitate research, experimentation, and A/B testing as required


Role Qualifications and Requirements

Bachelor degree and above in language, translation, localization or international studies, or equivalent experience

5+ years experience in a similar localization role in the online space

Proven experience in translation, localization and language quality management

Native level of French with fluent English

Experience managing linguistic teams and/or multiple Language Service Providers

Deep understanding of the end-to-end localization processes

Good knowledge of localization technology, incl. CATs, TMSs, MT and post-editing

Good understanding of internationalization with experience in product testing and bug reporting

Experience in creative writing, media or online marketing industry a plus

Excellent communication, presentation and interpersonal skills with ability to influence diverse stakeholders across business and leadership levels

Proven track of conflict resolution and mediation

Ability to show initiative, work independently, and drive initiatives at strategic level

Strong business acumen and analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and draw conclusions to make business decisions and drive actions


Inclusion at Booking.com:

Take it from our Chief People Officer, Paulo Pisano: “At Booking.com, the diversity of our people doesn’t just create a unique workplace, it also creates a better and more inclusive travel experience for everyone. Inclusion is at the heart of everything we do. It’s a place where you can make your mark and have a real impact in travel and tech.”

Career Development Opportunities:

Learn more about Your Career Journey here.

Become a Mentee and benefit from a mentoring relationship with a more experienced person to help you identify and achieve your professional and personal development goals.

About Us

Kielo Research is a boutique research consultancy specialising in patient-centred research services (patient preference studies, clinical outcomes assessments, qualitative interviews, surveys, etc.) for the pharmaceutical and medical device industries. We strive to deliver the highest-quality science that helps our clients bring life-changing treatments to patients worldwide.


What does Kielo (Senior) Research Data Analyst do?


Our (senior) research data analysts are members of scientific project teams and provide expertise for statistical analyses of health outcomes studies with patients, caregivers, and clinicians. This includes tasks such as:



Development of statistical and psychometric analysis plans
Formal experimental design of choice experiments (such as discrete choice experiments, best-worst scaling, thresholding, and swing weighting)
Statistical analysis, including econometric analysis of choice data (e.g. from discrete choice experiments) and psychometric analyses (classical and modern), using R
Writing statistical analysis sections to study protocols and reports
Support efficient study operations by developing scripts to automate manual research tasks


Our data analysts may also support projects in the role of (senior) research associates, with tasks such as:



Literature reviews
Protocol development
Conducting patient interviews, including telephone interviews and online or in-person focus group moderation
Survey implementation and testing
Qualitative coding and analysis
Writing study reports and scientific publications
Who are you?


We are looking for candidates with experience conducting statistical analyses of patient preference, clinical outcome assessment, or other health outcomes data in R. Familiarity with data management and visualisation using the tidyverse packages is essential. The minimum educational requirements are:



Master’s degree in a relevant scientific field (e.g., statistics, (health) economics, epidemiology, computer science, or management science) with three years of relevant experience; or
Doctoral degree in a relevant scientific field with one year of relevant experience


Any of the following experiences is considered an advantage:



Experience with the design or conduct of stated preference studies
Experience with psychometric analyses
Experience with survey scripting
Experience in publishing in peer-reviewed scientific journals


Kielo Research is a digital-enabled consultancy, and we expect our staff to be fluent in using Microsoft Office products and comfortable with digital workflows.


Fluency in English is essential. French, German, Spanish, or Italian is considered an advantage.


Why work for Kielo Research?


We apply innovative methods to capture patient insights that inform patient-centred drug development and lead methodological developments in the field. We invest heavily in the professional development of our staff and fund scientific conference visits and training. We offer attractive compensation packages and include substantial performance-based bonuses.


Kielo Research is based in Switzerland, the UK, and Germany, but our employees work remotely in various European locations. Our remote-first policy means that:



Our employees can choose flexibly where they work from
Our employees can obtain a co-working space at a suitable location, with costs covered by the company
We meet regularly in person at one of our employer locations in Europe


You could be based in the United Kingdom, Bulgaria, Croatia, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Latvia, Lithuania, the Netherlands, Poland, Portugal, Romania, or Sweden.


We currently offer full-time positions (100% FTE). The positions require 5% travel.

WebBeds is the fastest growing and most significant accommodation supplier to the travel industry. We are a global company offering ground services (hotels, transfers, tours, activities) to travel professionals. Our products help our partners and customers to create amazing Travel experiences.

Our Products range from a Retail Online platform, Integration Connectivity hub and distribution APIs to name but a few and our systems are 100% cloud hosted processing up to 5.0bn search requests daily. We partner with tens of thousands of companies across France , Spain , Belgium , and Canada , serving over a million members.

How we work: our Leadership Principles

Mission is the Boss — We think long-term and are methodical optimists who take risks, seeking our mission's success above all else.

Distributed Ownership — Accountable enlightened despots: everyone owns their decisions and results.

Radical Transparency — All information is accessible and written-first, so everyone can make the best decisions asynchronously.

Always Growing — Direct, positive, and caring feedback, combined with self-growth ownership.

The Brand Lead role

Context

In a context where Alan has surpassed one million members and positions itself as an integrated player in insurance, prevention, and access to healthcare—operating across four countries—we want to significantly boost our brand awareness and scale up our brand campaigns. This is why we’re recruiting a Brand Lead.

We’ve expanded across B2C, B2B, public sector, occupational medicine, and soon adjacent segments. We want to keep working on an unified brand narrative for the company.

We’re scaling fast across France, Spain, Belgium and Canada, with ambition to become N°1 health leader in Europe. To achieve this, we need a brand with a powerful positioning, that is flexible enough to work across different markets, cultural contexts, and levels of brand maturity.

We have great products to showcase with new product launches coming and a lovable brand that people trust.

We haven't done a major brand campaign since 2019.

Pillars of the role

The role revolves around four key pillars, which are as follows:

Define Alan’s strategic brand positioning

Dramatically grow brand awareness

Launch stand-out brand campaigns

Lead the Brand team

Your traits and achievements

Define Alan’s strategic brand positioning

Ability to deliver an incredible positioning: Proven track record of crafting distinctive, memorable brand positioning for complex multi-product companies.

High agency and decisiveness: Can cut through ambiguity, make bold strategic choices, and get stakeholders buy-in across countries and segments.

Translates positioning into tangible campaigns: Can translate Alan’s positioning into a clear set of campaign buckets (product launches, brand announcements, ambassadors, influencers…)

Dramatically grow brand awareness

Great taste and vision: Has led campaigns that clearly stood out creatively, with examples of work they’re proud of and can explain why it was distinctive.

Earns media ROI: Can generate organic momentum through social media or PR, and then scale it through broadcast media and paid, turning one idea into a true 360° campaign.

Data-driven decision maker: Is able to take full ownership of brand KPIs (awareness, consideration, branded search, etc.) and tracking tools to ensure a direct feedback loop between strategy, execution, and learnings.

Strategic thinking: Sees brand as a product, a system, a business asset, and a long-term defensive asset. Knows how to build a brand system actually adopted by Sales, Product, and Marketing teams.

Launch stand-out brand campaigns

Both strategic and hands-on: Can define campaign strategy but also roll up sleeves to execute, make creative decisions, and push work forward. Has personally owned end-to-end campaigns, from brief to post-mortem, with 1-2 concrete case studies where they can walk us through the problem, strategic choices, creative decisions, and results.

Led large-scale above the line campaigns: Has managed at least one sizable brand campaign budget (€1m+), in collaboration with creative agencies and media agencies, that drove a measurable lift in awareness and generated disproportionate attention.

Build and organise the brand team

Team builder and mentor: Has built brand teams before, can coach marketers, and enjoys developing people’s skills.

Operates in France, Spain or Belgium, and thinks globally: Knows our product, lives in our market, and can build relationships face-to-face with the team. Can shape a positioning and campaign approach that scales consistently across countries.

Expected outcomes

Deliver amazing brand campaigns that materially lift Alan’s brand in 2026 while clarifying our positioning for our different segments

Lead the brand team with high talent density and Alaners empowered to do their best work

This position may be open to exceptional remote profiles. Level range on our salary grid is E-G.

Perks & Benefits

At Alan, we believe that being in good health is a basic need, and it starts with our employees. This is why Alaners are provided with a stimulating environment and perks ensuring they are happy, efficient and spend only high-quality time with co-workers.

Therefore, we offer:

Fair rewards – Generous equity packages complement your base salary, for permanent contracts only

Flexible Office – Amazing office space at our Paris HQ, sponsored co-working hubs or a full-remote experience with home office equipment sponsorship, we want you to live where you're the happiest

All the tools you need – Top of the range equipment: Macbook Pro, keyboard, laptop stand, monitor, and Bose noise-canceling headphones

Flexible vacation policy and flexible working hours – Organize your time as you wish

Delightful healthcare insurance – Extremely comprehensive health insurance - 100% of the contribution covered by Alan for you and your family, for permanent contracts only

Transport – Country-specific commuter benefits

Learning & Training opportunities – A highly flexible Training policy, free books and budget to attend and speak at conferences if the opportunity arises

Parental leave – Extended parental leave for all new parents, for permanent contracts only

Important note: we hire people, not roles.

After reading this job description, if you feel like you don't have all the necessary prerequisites but that it matches where you are now or what you'd like to grow into in your next position, we encourage you to apply!

Not everybody will enter our recruitment process, but all, no matter how underrepresented, should feel free to apply as it can only bring learnings or success.

If you identify yourself as a woman: Did you know that research shows women often apply only when meeting 100% of requirements? Remember, this is just a guide, not a checklist. We'll be thrilled to receive your application!

Check out our About Alan and Career pages, as well as our Medium, blog and Glassdoor page for more info.

This is an in-person role based in Madrid. Full professional proficiency in English is mandatory.

The Programmatic Campaign Specialist is responsible for flawless, large-scale campaign execution across multiple programmatic platforms. This is a senior operational role focused entirely on execution—not client contact—and requires someone capable of managing 50+ campaigns simultaneously with precision, speed, and strategic insight.

This specialist owns every stage of campaign operations: pre-launch readiness, in-flight optimization, ongoing monitoring, and performance reporting. They also bring experience from both the buy-side and sell-side, understanding how agencies activate campaigns, what drives buyer behaviour, and how publisher setups can be optimized accordingly.

Curation experience and deep knowledge of GAM and Xandr are strong advantages.

Purpose of the Role

To ensure that programmatic campaigns launch flawlessly, run smoothly, and exceed performance expectations, while removing operational workload from Sales and Account Management. The role blends operational excellence with strategic insight and a deep understanding of both supply and demand dynamics.

Duties & Responsibilities

1\. Pre-Campaign Readiness & Launch Excellence


Own all pre-launch preparation across multiple platforms (GAM, Xandr, DSPs, SSPs).
Ensure every campaign is launch-ready: assets validated, trackers implemented, setups QA’d, KPIs clear.
Maintain a structured pre-launch checklist to avoid errors and guarantee punctual, polished launches.
Validate audience targeting, pacing logic, integrations, budget distribution, and technical tracking.
Align internally to secure best-in-class launch quality from day one.
2\. Campaign Execution, Monitoring & Optimization


Manage 50+ simultaneous campaigns across multiple programmatic platforms with full ownership.
Monitor delivery, pacing, and performance daily; troubleshoot proactively.
Apply both buy-side and sell-side thinking (buyer value vs. publisher monetization).
Use insights to rotate creatives, adjust targeting, optimize bids, and improve segmentation.
Diagnose anomalies and implement corrective actions immediately.
3\. Sales Support Through Operational Excellence


Take full ownership of execution, allowing Sales to focus on revenue and growth.
Create templated briefs, material requests, and spec sheets to streamline processes.
Communicate clearly, concisely, and with urgency.
4\. Reporting & Performance Insights


Produce reports that tell a clear story: what worked, what didn’t, why, and next steps.
Highlight top-performing creatives, audiences, formats, and placements.
Provide preemptive explanations for delivery issues or KPI shifts—no surprises.
Present results visually and strategically, aligned with business goals.
5\. Process Innovation, Automation & Tooling


Build and refine workflows that increase speed and reduce manual tasks.
Test new curation workflows, optimization tactics, and platform tools.
Create reusable templates for reporting, QA, setup, and optimization.
6\. Documentation, Knowledge Sharing & Internal Enablement


Maintain documentation of campaign best practices and troubleshooting guides.
Share learnings to elevate consistency and operational excellence across teams.
Support internal onboarding with clear training materials.
Qualifications:
Experience


Minimum 4+ years in programmatic advertising, campaign operations, or performance marketing.
Demonstrated ability to manage 50+ active campaigns across multiple platforms.
Hands-on expertise in GAM and Xandr — mandatory.
Experience on both buy-side and sell-side (agency activation + publisher operations).
Curation experience or familiarity with curated marketplaces is strongly preferred.
Strong operational and analytical skills with a bias toward action.
Technical Skills


Proficiency with programmatic platforms (DSPs, SSPs, ad servers, tracking systems).
Strong understanding of pacing, auction dynamics, programmatic KPIs, and optimization levers.
Proficiency in Excel/Sheets for analysis and reporting.
Familiarity with QA tools, troubleshooting methods, and workflow systems.
Bonus: experience building automations, scripts, or operational shortcuts.
Core Capabilities


Highly structured, proactive, and operationally disciplined.
Strong analytical judgement with the ability to turn data into clear, actionable insights.
Comfortable navigating both buy-side logic and sell-side monetization strategies.
Process-oriented with a passion for scalability, workflow design, and system building.
Clear, concise communicator with a solution-driven mindset.
Benefits

Wellness Hub (Wellhub): Access to 5,000+ gyms and wellness centers, with the option to add a friend or family member throughout 2026.
40 Days of Work From Home.
25 Vacation Days.
Free drinks, coffee, and snacks bar.

About the role

Our Financial Crime team blends regulatory expertise with data-driven thinking to make sure our products meet legal and policy requirements and deliver real value to customers. In a fast-moving, digital environment, they stay one step ahead by finding smart, scalable ways to prevent financial crime.

We're looking for an innovative and driven Crypto Financial Crime Compliance Manager to join the second line of defence. You'll design, lead, and shape the future of oversight and compliance by quickly identifying money laundering risks and maintaining systems and controls related to crypto.

Up to shape what's next in finance? Let's get in touch.

What you'll be doing

Maintaining and updating group FinCrime policies
Providing oversight and support to the identification, remediation, and resolution of FinCrime-related risk incidents
Assisting senior management in defining standards to manage financial crime risks, and ensuring that these standards are applied across the organisation
Offering 2LoD support to the business and 1LoD FinCrime teams with the launch of products, procedures, processes, and systems
Ensuring FinCrime risks are considered in our day-to-day operations, including in the development of new products and the taking-on of new customers
Supporting the maintenance of FinCrime training provided to employees
Participating in FinCrime-related governance committees to check-and-challenge initiative owners, identify potential issues, and propose solutions
Establishing, maintaining, and reviewing dashboards on an ongoing basis to identify areas that might fall outside risk appetite and which require further investigation
Preparing reports to senior management, group committees, and the Board, and escalating any key financial crime compliance issues

What you'll need

4+ years of experience in FinCrime (AML/CTF and sanctions), covering areas across retail and business, such as KYC, CDD/EDD, transaction monitoring, risk assessments, reporting (SARs), etc.
Experience working with crypto products
A good understanding of both innovative financial services products (crypto, buy now-pay later, etc.) and associated financial crime risks
Knowledge of anti-financial crime frameworks, including AML/CTF and sanctions policies
An understanding of regulatory recommendations, risk management practices, procedures, and control arrangements in complex financial organisations
Excellent knowledge of global requirements for financial crime controls
Knowledge of industry-wide financial crime practices and trends
Expertise in identifying financial crime risks and potential vulnerabilities, and advising on best practices to address those findings
A proven track record of stakeholder management, and the ability to collaborate, negotiate, and communicate effectively
Fluency in English

Nice to have

A professional qualification from an internationally recognised body, such as ICA or ACAMS
Excellent knowledge of key regulatory issues on anti-fraud and control assessment processes
A willingness to develop expertise in cryptocurrency regulations

Compensation range

Poland: PLN13,600 - PLN18,900 gross monthly*
Other locations: Compensation will be discussed during the interview process
Final compensation will be determined based on the candidate's qualifications, skills, and previous experience

Turn complex satellite data into insights that change how we see the world.

At SATLANTIS, we design advanced optical and space technologies to help solve global challenges from above. From our headquarters in Leioa, we lead innovation in high-resolution imaging systems and data processing for Earth observation and space missions.

You’ll join our Image Processing team to develop algorithms that transform raw satellite data into precise, reliable information used in real operational missions.



What’s in it for you

Impactful innovation: Contribute to technologies that transform satellite images into environmental, scientific, and strategic insights with real-world applications.

Challenging projects: Work on advanced image processing algorithms and models used in operational space missions and international observation programs.

Growth and autonomy: Collaborate with experts in optics, software, and data analysis while leading projects that shape the future of space imaging.

Flexible working hours: Manage your schedule with autonomy and trust to deliver your best work.

Private health insurance: Your wellbeing matters, supported by comprehensive healthcare coverage.

Dynamic, collaborative environment: Join a multidisciplinary team where innovation, precision, and creativity drive every mission.



Your mission

Your mission will be to design, implement, and optimize image-processing algorithms that transform raw satellite data into accurate, actionable information used in SATLANTIS optical payloads and Earth-observation missions.

Through your work, you’ll help validate imaging systems, improve image quality, and reduce technical risks in space missions—contributing directly to how we observe and understand our planet.

In this role, you will:

Design, develop, and optimize image-processing algorithms for satellite data analysis and simulation.

Implement radiometric, geometric, and multiband alignment corrections to enhance image quality and consistency.

Validate algorithms using real mission and laboratory data to ensure accuracy, robustness, and operational performance.



What will set you up for success

Proven experience in scientific or analytical image processing: You bring at least 2–3 years of hands-on experience developing image processing solutions, with strong proficiency in Python and libraries such as OpenCV, NumPy, SciPy, TensorFlow/PyTorch, or equivalent.

Analytical mindset and problem-solving skills: You’re able to translate complex data challenges into effective technical solutions that deliver measurable results.

Knowledge of satellite or geospatial data: Familiarity with remote sensing principles, calibration methods, or sensor modeling will help you contribute from day one.

Interest in advanced imaging systems is a plus: Experience with TDI (Time Delay Integration) sensors, optical modelling, simulation pipelines, or image-quality metrics (SNR, MTF, PRNU/DSNU) is a strong advantage and will allow you to work on some of our most advanced payloads.

Collaborative communication: You thrive in multidisciplinary and international environments, communicating fluently in English and Spanish to build strong technical and human connections.

Curiosity and drive for improvement: You continuously seek new approaches, optimize performance, and challenge the status quo to push innovation forward.

Ready to take your next step in the space industry?

If you’re driven by the challenge of transforming satellite data into knowledge, enjoy solving complex image processing problems, and want to contribute to cutting-edge optical technology that impacts the world, apply now and join SATLANTIS’ mission to advance the future of space imaging.

Are you passionate about turning data into strategic insight? This is a rare and exciting opportunity for a Market Intelligence Manager to join Hitachi's Global Marketing & Sales (GM&S) team, a high-profile corporate initiative at the very center of the Hitachi Group.


As a Market Intelligence Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping our global strategy. You’ll dive deep into market trends, competitor landscapes, customer needs to uncover opportunities that drive real business impact, and bring the voice of the marketplace into Hitachi to enable an agile, customer-centric growth organisation


About GM&S

Hitachi is transforming to ensure sustainable growth and profitability in digital and green sectors by establishing a customer-centric business model. Part of this transformation is setting up global account management to better understand customer needs and increase Hitachi's market share.

This transformation is led by GM&S, whose vision is to become our customers’ preferred partner of choice for their most critical challenges by providing impactful, sustainable solutions, delivered by experts spanning the full breadth of Hitachi’s portfolio.


What you’ll be doing:


Take ownership of specific areas of market intelligence, whether it’s segment insights or account profiling, you’ll lead the charge.


Conduct market sizing and trend analysis across key sectors like Mobility and Data Centers.


Build detailed profiles of global and regional accounts, identifying their strategies, challenges, and where Hitachi can win.


Analyse competitors’ performance, strategies, and product pipelines to inform our positioning.


Support strategic initiatives such as go-to-market planning, M&A scoping, and economic/trade analysis.


Collaborate with cross-functional teams to embed insights into business strategies and foster customer-first thinking.


Present your findings in compelling ways through presentations, workshops, and working sessions with senior stakeholders.


Manage relationships with external research vendors, ensuring high standards and ethical research practices.


Contribute to the evolution of our AI-powered insights tool.


What you’ll bring to the team:


At least 10 years of professional experience, including 5+ years in market research.


A proven ability to thrive in complex, fast-changing environments.


Strong numerical and analytical skills - you’re comfortable working with unstructured data and extracting meaningful insights.

Experience with annual planning processes.


A strategic mindset with a talent for identifying the “so what” behind the data.


Excellent communication skills in English (Japanese is a plus).


Experience in a strategy consultancy or market research agency is a bonus, but not essential.


About Hitachi


Since its founding in 1910, Hitachi has supported the development of society and the improvement of people's lives. Throughout the world, Hitachi Group employees exemplify outstanding teamwork that transcends the boundaries of geographical regions and business fields. Together, we share the Hitachi group identity and put it into practice worldwide.


With a mission to deliver the best experience to employees and customers, you will be joining a global team setting the standard for excellence and innovation in Marketing and Sales (M&S).


In pursuit of sustainable growth and profitability in the digital and green sectors, the Global Marketing & Sales transformation team, established in 2022, seeks to position Hitachi as a trusted partner for its customers.


Our values


We are proud to say we are an equal opportunity employer and welcome all applicants for employment without attention to any factor that doesn’t impact your ability to do the job, including race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, veteran or disability status.

We are proud of Japanese heritage, with our values expressed through the Hitachi Spirit:


Wa – Harmony, Trust, Respect


Makoto – Sincerity, Fairness, Honesty, Integrity


Kaitakusha-Seishin – Pioneering Spirit, Challenge


If, like us, you’re motivated by delivering first class services, thrive in a fast paced and supportive environment and want to help Hitachi drive social innovation, we’d love to hear from you.

Experience or interest in Change Management within S4H environments is a plus.

Certifications in Agile, Design Thinking, or HR transformation methodologies are a bonus but not required.

Your Winning Qualities:

Exposure to transformation programs involving strategy, stakeholder engagement, communication, and training.

Ability to support project management activities, including tracking and reporting, ideally with Agile methodologies.

Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to collaborate effectively and build relationships.

A proactive team player who thrives in a collaborative and intellectually stimulating environment.

A creative problem solver with a structured and analytical mindset.

What’s in It for You:

Join our Talent & Organization practice for a dynamic and rewarding career where diverse opportunities allow you to grow professionally every day. You will work alongside more than 300 clients in the Fortune 500 globally across 40+ industries in the areas of HR transformation, learning, and organizational effectiveness.

We offer a competitive salary, an extensive benefits package that includes additional vacation days, medical and life insurance, a supportive culture focused on continuous learning, and much more!

Alantra Enagas Energy Transition and its 210M€ Klima fund overview:

The Klima Energy Transition Fund, seeded, among other investors, by Enagas, the Spanish gas grid operator, is focussing on backing experienced and agile management teams to accelerate the deployment of innovative solutions, aiming at solving the energy transition equation. The fund takes minority stakes in late stage ventures and early growth companies in the following sectors: (1) low carbon solutions (hydrogen, biogas, power to gas, …), (2) mobility (EVs but also heavy duty and maritime), (3) energy storage (batteries, alternative technologies, developers, …), (4) smart grid (sensors and data, flexibility, …) , (5) incremental technologies for renewable power generation (sensors, software, drones, offshore wind,…), (6) power markets (PPA platforms, add-ons to ETRM, green traders, market places …), with two transversal topics: digital solutions and energy efficiency. The fund of 210M€ invest mainly across Europe but also in North America, tickets of 6 to 10 M€ per company and usually is represented at board level.

The team of 7 who is managing the fund is based in Madrid, London, Paris and Bonn, all coming from the direct investment industry and 5 of them spending a number of years helping start-ups and SMEs in the energy field to grow faster. Product Manager, High Frequency Trading

Location: Remote, local to regional Keyrock hub locations

About Keyrock

Since our beginnings in 2017, we've grown to be a leading change-maker in the digital asset space, renowned for our partnerships and innovation.

Today, we partner with over 180 team members around the world. Our diverse team hails from 42 nationalities, with backgrounds ranging from self-taught DeFi natives to PhDs. Predominantly remote, we have hubs in London, Brussels, and Singapore, and host regular online and offline hangouts to keep the crew tight.

We are trading on more than 80 exchanges, and working with a wide array of asset issuers. As a well-established market maker, our distinctive expertise led us to expand rapidly. Today, our services span market making, options trading, high-frequency trading, OTC, and DeFi trading desks.

But we’re more than a service provider. We’re an initiator. We're pioneers in adopting the Rust Development language for our algorithmic trading, and champions of its use in the industry. We support the growth of Web3 startups through our Accelerator Program. We upgrade ecosystems by injecting liquidity into promising DeFi, RWA, and NFT protocols. And we push the industry's progress with our research and governance initiatives.

At Keyrock, we're not just envisioning the future of digital assets. We're actively building it.

Mission statement

We are seeking a highly skilled Product Manager to oversee our High-Frequency Trading (HFT) product vertical. This role is critical to our success, focusing on the discovery, design, and delivery of trading systems where every microsecond counts.

You will be responsible for driving the product strategy for our HFT systems, ensuring sub-millisecond latency and near-perfect uptime, while balancing aggressive performance goals with robust risk management and regulatory compliance. You will act as the crucial link between our elite trading teams and our world-class engineers, translating complex quantitative needs into a clear, actionable roadmap that directly impacts our P&L and market share.

Keyrock Product Managers are responsible for developing valuable and viable systems (& implicit services) that consider the voices of

Business & Commercial teams,

Trading & Research teams,

And Engineering builders.

The position requires a versatile skill set across these three competencies, enabling candidates to tackle research responsibilities with confidence.

Outcomes

Low-Latency System Leadership: Drive the focus, design, and continuous improvement of HFT systems, ensuring the achievement and maintenance of sub-millisecond latency for critical trading operations.

High-Availability Operations: Be accountable for achieving and maintaining 99.99% uptime for all critical trading systems and their underlying infrastructure, ensuring robust and reliable performance.

Commercial Impact & Strategy: Shape and drive product activities with a relentless focus on commercial impact, directly contributing to P&L, market share, and capital efficiency targets.

Continuous Product Innovation: Lead the discovery, design, and development of ongoing trading enhancements and risk monitoring tools, ensuring our systems maintain a competitive edge.

Strategic Roadmapping & Communication: Develop, own, and clearly communicate the product roadmap, ensuring tight alignment between traders, engineering, and business stakeholders on all projected work.

System Resilience & Validation: Oversee the rigorous stress-testing of trading systems and ensure their seamless integration into back-testing frameworks to validate performance and robustness.

Regulatory & Compliance Alignment: Define and integrate compliance and regulatory requirements into the product roadmap, ensuring all systems operate within legal and internal risk frameworks.

Market & Vendor Intelligence: Manage key relationships with data providers and exchanges while continuously scanning the competitive landscape to identify strategic threats and opportunities.

Stakeholder Nexus: Act as the central communication and management hub between traders, quants, engineers, and other business stakeholders, ensuring focus is maintained on delivering impact and value.

Essentials

Low-Latency Systems Experience: Demonstrable exposure to the product management or development of low-latency or high-frequency trading systems.

Quantitative Acumen: Strong quantitative and analytical skills, with the ability to understand and discuss complex financial models and trading strategies.

Technical Proficiency: A deep technical understanding of the components of a modern electronic trading system, from market data ingestion to order execution.

Trading & Risk Management Exposure: Solid understanding of financial trading principles and sophisticated risk management practices in a high-stakes environment.

Proven Product Execution: A proven track record of translating complex business and quantitative needs into clear, actionable technology requirements for engineering teams.

Beneficial experience

Deep understanding of market microstructure, particularly within crypto markets.

A genuine passion for and deep interest in digital assets and crypto trading.

Experience with modern, agile product management methodologies with a focus on measuring and optimizing delivery efficiency.

A background in software development or infrastructure engineering, especially with real-time or high-performance systems.

Familiarity with the regulatory compliance landscape for financial markets.

Previous experience in the fintech sector, particularly with derivatives markets.

Our recruitment philosophy

We value self-awareness in our recruitment process. We seek people who understand themselves and their career goals. We're after those with the right skills and a conscious choice to join our field. The perfect fit? A crypto enthusiast who’s driven, collaborative, and delivers solid, scalable outcomes.

Why Keyrock?

Be at the forefront of innovation in digital assets and AI.

Work on challenging problems with a direct impact on the business.

A competitive salary package, with various benefits depending on the method of engagement (employee or freelancer).

Autonomy in your time management thanks to flexible working hours and the opportunity to work remotely

The freedom to create your own entrepreneurial experience by being part of a team of people in search of excellence

As an employer we are committed to build an inclusive, diverse and non-discriminating work environment. We welcome employees of all backgrounds, ethnicities, genders, creed and sexual orientation. We hire, reward and promote entirely based on merit and performance.

Due to the nature of our business and external requirements, we perform background checks on all potential employees, passing which is a prerequisite to join Keyrock.

Verisure and our values

We Are People Who Protect People

We are the leading provider of professionally monitored alarms for residential households and small businesses in Europe.

Peace of mind for families and small businesses

We believe it is a human right to feel safe and secure. We now protect more than 4 million families and small businesses. Our service includes 24/7 monitoring, expert verification and response, customer care, service and maintenance services, and professional technical support. As well as protecting against intrusion, we protect against fire, attack, theft, life-threatening emergencies and other hazards. Verisure people are dedicated and committed and provide what we believe is the best customer service in the industry.

Our business is driven by organic growth, based on our differentiated business model. We attract high quality customers. We work hard to ensure that our customers are happy. This contributes to an industry leading level of attrition and a long customer lifetime.

Our 18,000 team-mates are the foundation of our company. Our business model combines technology and human expertise to protect people. We are a human services company. More than 80% of our colleagues interact with customers every day. This connection provides the insight that inspires our innovation. These insights are a source of advantage. We leverage these insights with the significant investment we make into product and service innovation. The close customer contact also fuels the pride which drives engagement.

30 years of experience – and many more to come

We have been protecting what matters most since 1988. Over that time, we have expanded beyond our roots in the Nordics and Spain. We now serve most of Europe and have also established a strong presence in Latin America. In 2017, to evolve our operating model, strategic innovation, operational excellence and access to talent, we opened our new Group headquarters in Geneva, Switzerland. In 2019, we formed a strategic partnership with Arlo, a world leader in connected cameras. Verisure acquired all rights to Arlo’s European business. This allows us to offer connected camera services in Europe as a complement to our professionally monitored security service. It also allows us to access new go-to-market opportunities in retail and e-commerce.

Everything we do is driven by our DNA. Our DNA has enabled us to grow at a double-digit pace for many years and is setting us up to continue this journey.

Passionate in Everything We Do: Our people have a sense of energy that is unmistakable, one that drives us to delight our customers and focus on creating impact quickly.

Committed to Making a Difference: When we say we will do something; we deliver with excellence. We are accountable, focused and operate with discipline.

Always Innovating: We believe that Innovation can be big or small; it’s a continuous state of mind that inspires us to think differently and always make things better. We are risk takers and we learn continuously.

Winning as a Team: Our people know that by leveraging one another’s strengths, investing in and developing our team’s capability and by collaborating well, we will win.

With Trust & Responsibility: Operating with integrity is core to our success. We are humble, honest and value deep mastery and expertise. We do the right thing, always.

Your analysis will form the fundamental basis of expert advice that will be given to clients, competition authorities and/or courts. Research Analysts join us after completing their undergraduate degree in a quantitative subject. This would preferably be in economics, but we also welcome applications from graduates with degrees in computer science, mathematics or statistics.

Contexte

Dans le cadre du développement de nos projets digitaux, nous recherchons un Product Owner capable d’intervenir sur des sujets e-commerce transverses (parcours client, back-office, intégrations) et/ou d’accompagner des projets data produits autour des référentiels de type MDM/PIM/DAM.

As part of the Process Automation team within the Operations Technology function, the Product Coordinator for Auto-gate Systems plays a key role in current auto-gate systems stabilization, problem solving and being involved in deployments. Additionally, the role will drive standardization across terminals, managing auto-gate requirements, controlling all the gate scope components, identifying possible risks and deviations and giving support during all product live phases. Coordination capabilities internally within the process automation product team and externally cross functionally with other teams by applying product management, communication, design, systems and strategic thinking, industrial internet of things (industry 4.0) best practices during the product life cycle.

Lead fact based and data driven root cause analysis following world class practices (Lean Six Sigma, Statistical Process Control) to stabilize and improve product performance where needed
Promote the delivery of solution demonstrations and technical training to terminal teams at any time during the different stages of product life cycle either because of product deployment, product iteration, features implementation, etc. This includes reviewing product training materials and making sure are according to the scope and specific product requirements, aiming to improve the adoption of the product at terminal level
Create, maintain and continuously improve all relevant product documentation, policies, and technical standards. GROUP BNP PARIBAS

BNP Paribas Group is the top bank in the European Union and a major international banking establishment. It has close to 185,000 employees in 65 countries. In Spain we are more than 5,100 employees within 13 business lines.


RISK HUB
RISK is an integrated and independent control function of the BNP Paribas Group. It is the second line of defense on the risk management activities of the Group which are under its direct responsibilities, including credit and counterparty risk, market risk, funding and liquidity risk, interest rate and foreign exchange risks in the banking book, insurance risk, operational risk, and environmental and social risks.
RISK aims at being a partner of the businesses by contributing to their sustainable development, but also a gatekeeper to ensure risks taken remain compatible with the Group’s Risk Appetite and its strategy.
RISK Iberian Hub Madrid is a transversal platform servicing the RISK Function by covering added-value activities around credit risk, market risk, operational risk and data protection. Offering a wide range of services to RISK teams, from consulting to cyber security going through data analysis, modelling or artificial intelligence.


ABOUT THE JOB


Want to help shape the bank of tomorrow today?

How can the Bank leverage data to assess credit risk on its portfolio of existing and new clients?

Risk assessment encompasses various elements: how likely is it that a client will not be able to comply with the contractual requirements of his loan? What will be the client’s outstanding balance in this situation? What loss is the bank expected to suffer if a client is no longer able to repay a loan

As a member of the RISK Models team, you will join a team of experts whose goal is to answer these questions through the development of statistical or expert models.

By analysing historical data, working together with the business lines, IT department, the modelling experts at BNP Paribas group and taking into account the remarks of supervisor representatives, the team creates tools that help the bank calculate the capital requirements for its credit exposure, the risk component of the credit price or that contribute to the approval process of a new credit.



RESPONSIBILITIES


Your personal tasks will be:

Designing and developing models for credit risk on the scope mainly of companies.
Finding, managing and using the most appropriate data sources for modelling purposes.
Working with expert colleagues and business representatives to examine the results and keep models grounded in reality.
Documenting each step of the development and informing decision makers by presenting them options and results.
Ensuring correct implementation of the tools (together with the IT department).
Continuously assessing models by means of back-testing.
Answering specific, external requests regarding statistics related to credit risk assessment.
Following-up evolutions in the regulation and in credit risk modelling best-practices.

Studies
Master degree or higher in Mathematics, Economy or Econometrics, Statistics or a similar background where analytics and figures prevail.
Experience
Work experience of at least 3 years in credit risk modelling (PD/LGD/LGDD/CCF)

You express yourself perfectly in English, in particular in the writing form. You ideally speak French fluently


SKILLS

Transversal & Behavioral

Titulo: Basque Language Specialist - Freelance AI Trainer Project
Descripcion_Completa:
Are you a Basque language expert eager to shape the future of AI? Large-scale language models are evolving from clever chatbots into powerful engines of linguistic discovery. With high-quality training data, tomorrow's AI can democratize world-class education, keep pace with cutting-edge research, and streamline communication for Basque speakers everywhere. That training data begins with you—we need your expertise to help power the next generation of AI.

We're looking for Basque language specialists who live and breathe Basque grammar, syntax, morphology, spelling, phonology, and pragmatics. You'll challenge advanced language models on topics like ergativity, verb conjugation, noun declension, sentence structure, dialectal variation, formal and informal speech, and the nuances of the Basque writing system—documenting every failure mode so we can harden model reasoning.

On a typical day, you will converse with the model on language scenarios, verify factual accuracy and logical soundness, capture reproducible error traces, and suggest improvements to our prompt engineering and evaluation metrics.

A master's or PhD in Basque language, linguistics, or a closely related field is ideal; peer-reviewed publications, teaching experience, or hands-on linguistic analysis projects signal fit. Clear, metacognitive communication—"showing your work"—is essential.

Ready to turn your Basque language expertise into the knowledge base for tomorrow's AI? Apply today and start teaching the model that will teach the world.

We offer a pay range of $6 to $65 per hour, with the exact rate determined after evaluating your experience, expertise, and geographic location. Final offer amounts may vary from the pay range listed above. As a contractor, you'll supply a secure computer and high-speed internet; company-sponsored benefits such as health insurance and PTO do not apply.

WHAT YOU WILL DO

This position offers excellent career and growth opportunities for talented, highly motivated professionals with relevant prior experience in a quantitative setting.

Flexible working and market leading work life balance will be a priority. There will be opportunities to travel with a mix of around 50% of time spent on client site vs off site, though individual needs will be considered when staffing offsite vs onsite roles.

A Team Leader would typically work on one or more projects at a time

Project work typically involves design and implementation of data products from both analytical and software points of view. Client presentations and interaction for decision making related to technical aspects of the project

Using Partners and the internal network effectively to provide technical and senior-level steer to projects

Working in a team together with clients to complete project deliverables to high standard

Maintaining energy and positive work life balance of team

Coaching Oliver Wyman and client team members to develop their skills

WHY JOIN US?

Come aboard if you are excited by challenges and want to work across different cultures .

We want you to step out of your comfort zone and embrace your curiosity .

Our consultants learn on the job and receive formal training to develop their comm unication , presentation , analytical , and client management skills .

You’ll find genuine colleagues who stand by their beliefs and measure success by the lasting impact you create together .

We believe in flexibility , including the opportunity of hybrid working . You are also invited to join our employee resource groups and social activities .

We’ll ask you to be brave, challenging the status quo and constantly striving to build something new to shape our firm and the world around us .

You’ll be a contributing team member from the start , building trust- based relationships with stakeholders and delivering breakthrough impact .

Your learning curve will be steep , with each project offering new opportunities to expand your toolkit and to team with specialists who have deep subject -matter and technical expertise .

You’ll likely have the chance to travel within your home market and internationally . No two weeks are ever the same.

Chart your course; we support the journey

You’ll have the opportunity to work on projects across all our industries and capabilities and will be able to specialize early or leave it until several years in.

Various colleagues will guide and coach you throughout your career, including a dedicated buddy , a career adviser, and y our talent manager.

We care deeply about sustainable work-life quality and provide for career flexibility with a variety of programs including sabbaticals, non-profit fellowships, and externships.

We hire you to be you

Our open, inclusive, and down-to-earth culture will enable you to bring your best authentic self to work.

You’ll work alongside down-to-earth colleagues who do serious work, but don’t take themselves too seriously.

There’s no corporate mold to fit and hierarchy doesn’t get in the way .

We do not let artificial barriers stand in the way of your personal career progression.

Associate Team Leader - At least 3 years of previous working experience
Team Leader - At least 5 years of previous working experience

Master’s degree in a quantitative discipline (desirable). Ideally Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Computer Science, Informatics, Data Science or, Engineering

Strong analysis, documentation, and communication skills

Experience in using advanced analytics such as Python (must), Matlab and R (desirable) and data manipulation softwares such as SAS and SQL.

Experience with data products development and productization at scale: development lifecycle, maintenance, agile methodologies.

Work effectively and collaboratively in a team.

Be independent and work under limited supervision respecting defined timelines.

Present results effectively, highlighting potential issues and implications.

Excellent command of Spanish and English language (verbal and written)

Ability to manage a demanding work volume and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment

Work Where It’s Best for You

Work Anywhere means you can work in an office or at home on any given day. It’s about getting the work done in the way and place that works best for each person. This applies across all locations and departments.

Work Where It’s Best for You

Work Anywhere means you can work in an office or at home on any given day. It’s about getting the work done in the way and place that works best for each person. This applies across all locations and departments.

Work Anywhere does not mean work at any time. We have predictable core hours where employees are generally available for meetings and collaboration. Employees are focused and available during core hours.

We invest in our offices to make them places where our employees like to go. If you work in the office three or more days a week, you will have a dedicated office workspace. Our offices function as hubs to draw people in, create social bonds, and where random connections and mixing of ideas happen. We’re investing more in offices, culture, and offsite meetings, not less.

Product teams are organized in regional product hubs for optimal collaboration and live within a time zone of their hub. Our current product hubs are located in Pleasanton, Columbus, Boston, Kansas City, New York City, Raleigh, and Toronto. We create opportunities for teams to get together in person regularly.

Customer-facing roles, such as Sales and Professional Services, live near and/or travel to their customers.

When an employee moves within a country it does not cause a change in salary. Where you live impacts you and your family. Not knowing if your compensation will change if you move can cause stress and uncertainty for everyone. We wanted to eliminate that.

Work at Veeva. Work where it’s best for you.

System Administrator Role Overview

A System Administrator is responsible for managing and maintaining an organization’s IT infrastructure, focusing on servers, storage, operating systems, and data protection. The role ensures that systems operate efficiently and securely to meet business needs.

Key Responsibilities
1. Operating System Administration
Install, configure, and maintain operating systems (Windows, Linux, macOS) on servers and desktops.
Manage system updates, security patches, and software installations.
Monitor system performance and resolve OS-related issues.
2. Storage, Backup, and Recovery
Manage storage systems such as SAN, NAS, and cloud storage.
Implement backup strategies to ensure data integrity and availability.
Design and execute disaster recovery plans to ensure business continuity in case of system failures.
3. Hardware Support
Maintain and troubleshoot physical hardware, including servers, desktops, and peripherals.
Manage/perform server hardware setup, replacement, software and hardware upgrades, including security patching.
Oversee hardware procurement and configuration for organizational needs.
4. Security Management
Enforce security protocols, including access controls, encryption, and monitoring.
Identify vulnerabilities and apply patches to protect systems from security threats.
Monitor logs and system activity for any unusual or malicious behavior.
5. Architecture and Design Support
Support all architecture and design changes.
Perform implementation activities and execute testing tasks related to disaster recovery of EPO (Enterprise Policy Orchestrator).
Qualifications
Education
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related field.
Certifications
LINUX administration certification.
Microsoft Certified Systems Administrator (MCSA).
SAN administration certification or relevant certifications specific to EPO technologies.
Skills
Technical Skills
Strong understanding of operating systems (Windows, Linux, macOS).
Expertise in storage management and backup solutions.
Proficiency in scripting (e.g., PowerShell, Bash).
Hardware troubleshooting and maintenance.
Additional Knowledge & Skills
Problem Solving: Ability to analyze and solve technical issues efficiently.
Communication: Clear and patient communication, both written and verbal, to guide users through resolutions.
Customer Service: Focus on delivering a positive user experience.
Time Management: Ability to prioritize and manage multiple support tickets simultaneously.
Adaptability: Flexibility in learning new technologies and handling different types of issues.
Team Collaboration: Work effectively with other IT professionals and departments.
Attention to Detail: Accuracy in documenting issues and following up on tasks.
Experience
3-5 years of experience in system administration.
Experience in a customer-facing role is required.
Operational Framework
Familiarity with Operational Services Framework 2 (OSF 2) and its technical conditions.

Job Description


As you contemplate your future, you might be asking yourself, what’s the next step? Start your journey with us!

We’re seeking an experienced Senior Data Scientist to join our team in Barcelona. If you’re ready to soar, we’re ready to take you there.

You will have the opportunity to crunch massive datasets using the latest Big Data technologies as well as solve problems using state of the art AI algorithms. You will have the chance to debate and explore new ideas, approaches and technologies with your team to design and implement the best solutions. We follow the philosophy of using the right tool for the right job and are not afraid to explore the greenfield. You will be a key player in the organisation; designing and implementing advanced analytics and using machine learning to build AI powered solutions for almost every area in the business like online fraud detection, recommendation engines, user experience personalization, optimising the flight combinations we offer or the marketing campaigns we launch, and virtually every possible area subject to optimization in the business. Join us and help us reach our ambitious goal of becoming the global leader in online travel!

Within the Data Science team, we are focussed on researching and building cutting-edge solutions to evolve better travel experiences for our customers, all by leveraging the information from over 30 million searches that we receive everyday! Due to this scale, we are able to have a wider and better selection of travel plans that are tailored to suit the traveller’s needs which is probably more than any other travel site.. Machine learning models in production make over 1.8 Billion predictions a day improving customer experience and helping drive better decisions in an ever changing environment. The team is constantly evolving the next generation of models using cutting edge algorithms and technology to improve our goals to drive value for our customers.

Why eDreams ODIGEO
Join the world’s leading travel subscription platform and one of the largest e-commerce businesses in Europe.

Millions of customers every year across 44 markets – 5 brands – 7M members in Prime

More than 100 million searches per day – Up to 6 billion daily AI predictions[e1]

Over 1,600 employees – More than 60 different nationalities from all continents – 99% permanent contracts – 36 average age

We’re the world’s leading travel subscription platform and one of the largest e-commerce businesses in Europe. We are a tech company revolutionising the travel booking experience through our consumer insight, innovative technology, market leadership, and Prime, the world’s first travel subscription program.

What you will do:
The Role’s Key Responsibilities and Tasks

As an eDOer, you will have clear objectives, great challenges and a clear overview of how your work contributes to the global company project and its customers. As a Senior Data Scientist in the Data Science team you will be in charge of:

Work with multi-disciplinary teams and key stakeholders to drive value and growth for both our customers and business.

Take ownership of initiatives and ensure successful delivery from initiation to final outcome.

Provide transparency on initiatives to the team and stakeholders.

Influence and advice stakeholders on the best course of action.

Keep up-to-date on the advancements in the area of machine learning and AI.

Lead and mentor data scientists.

Act as an evangelist for machine learning and AI.

Adhere to company values, create and maintain a friendly working environment.

What you need to succeed:

Job Requirements
Bring your unique perspective, speak up, and offer disruptive solutions. You’ll have the opportunity to learn and grow while making a real impact on our team. Here’s what you need to succeed:

Degree in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Physics, Engineering or other quantitative fields);

5+ years of related experience as a Data Scientist, AI expert or Machine Learning engineer;

A solid understanding of relevant statistical, data processing and machine learning methods and their implementation using standard open source frameworks;

Experienced with building production ready machine learning solutions and MLOps;

Experienced Python developer. Other programming languages are a plus;

Experience with cloud platforms and scalable solutions;

Experience creating solutions powered by Generative AI, specially Large Language Models, and LLM frameworks like LangChain will be a plus;

Written and oral communication skills (in English).

Preferred Qualifications

Experience with Applied AI Tools: Demonstrated comfort using practical AI tools such as GitHub Copilot, ChatGPT, or other AI-powered coding assistants.

Experimentation Mindset: Curiosity and eagerness to explore, experiment with, and integrate emerging AI-driven solutions into software development workflows.

AI-Enhanced Problem Solving: Ability to effectively leverage AI tools to debug code, streamline development processes, and enhance productivity.

Adaptability and Learning Agility: Enthusiastic about continuously learning and quickly adapting to new AI features and capabilities.

Collaboration with AI: Experience or interest in collaborating closely with AI tools as “pair programmers” to complement traditional software development practices.

At eDO, we want you to be a part of our success story and great culture.

A rewarding Compensation package!
Prime Plus membership, competitive salary and benefits package, including flexible benefits, performance-based bonuses, birthday day off, discounts and partnerships, relocation support and the possibility of choosing your equipment and, even better, keeping it for free after 3 years.

Your Well-being is our priority. Embrace Freedom and Flexibility!
